t_ch: 瞬间，茶杯中茶水便活了过来，化作一团小小的水球浮在半空之中。铁皮囚犯瞬间反应过来，丁零当啷，小小走廊里所有铁器就兀自飞起，冲向饮茶青年。
t_en: The tea in the cup came alive in an instant, forming a small watery orb floating in midair. The prisoner reacted in kind, and in a clattering cacophony, all the iron objects in the narrow corridor hurtled towards the tea-drinking youth.

t_ch: 这一杯茶水游若蛟龙，以极快的速度弹开了所有飞行刑具，弹射力道之强劲，足以将碎裂的铁具深深嵌进墙内。
t_en: The tea swirled like a serpentine dragon, batting away all the iron pieces with such inhuman speed and force that they were wedged in the walls from the powerful deflections.

t_ch: 随后这杯茶水再次汇集，再次化作一团小小的水球。铁皮囚犯大惊失色，心说不好，下意识里想要择路而逃。此时这团茶水再次动作，封住了囚犯口鼻。
t_en: Then the tea gathered and formed into a watery orb once more. The prisoner, taken aback, thought to cut his losses and retreat. But the tea made its move again, sealing the prisoner's nostrils and mouth shut.

t_ch: 「咕咚」一声，这铁皮彪形大汉就倒在地上昏迷不醒了。
t_en: With a "thud," the towering iron-skinned figure fell to the ground, unconscious.

t_ch: 饮茶青年缓缓吐出「这里还不是最深处」，便消失在阴影之中。
t_en: The tea-drinking youth evenly muttered, "This is not the lowest depths," and disappeared into the darkness.

t_ch: 给我讲一段「云上五骁」吧。
t_en: Tell me something from the High-Cloud Quintet.

t_ch: 既然是客人要求，那我就讲一段「无罅飞光」的故事。
t_en: If the customer wishes for it, then allow me to regale you with a tale from the Transcendent Flash.

t_ch: 咱们得说到那一年云骑征兵日，校兵场上人头攒动。云骑士兵将场地中央的擂台围了个里三层、外三层，可以说是水泄不通。
t_en: We have to start off with that day of the year when Cloud Knights draft soldiers. The parade square was bustling with movement. Rows upon rows of Cloud Knights Soldiers surrounded a boxing ring in the middle of the square, forming a veritable moat.

t_ch: 这座擂台足有一丈五尺多高，擂台上站着是一位凶神恶煞、面带伤疤的擂官。这人来历非凡，和台下的入伍新兵截然不同。
t_en: This ring towered over fifteen feet and in it stood a ringmaster, his visage scarred and brutish. This man was tempered in battle, a stark contrast to those greenhorns below the ring.

t_ch: 「各位将士们！在下是擂官哞阿，原本是想来仙舟罗浮参加剑首大会，不料算错了星际时间，所以特设此擂台，希望能和将士们一分高下。」
t_en: "Soldiers! I am ringmaster Mou'A. I intended to come to the Xianzhou Luofu to partake in the Sword Champion summit, but I miscalculated the intergalactic time. So, I have specially built this ring, in which I hope to compare my might to you soldiers."

t_ch: 原来这彪形大汉，穿越星门而来，只为在刀剑功夫上争个高低。历代剑首大会上都有域外剑客参战，他们大多武艺高强、身怀绝技。
t_en: This burly fellow had traversed the interstellar portals merely to pit his skills in a martial competition. There would always be such foreign swordmasters in past Sword Champion summits who would participate in duels, and many of them would be extremely skilled.

t_ch: 「这刀剑无眼，当着云骑军众将士的面，我若是击杀打擂人，不合礼数，必然偿命。」大汉指着擂台上一尊陨铁靶人说道，「我就比谁的一剑更厉害！」
t_en: "The blade is blind. If I were to slay any challengers before the Cloud Knights, it does not fit decorum and I would be forfeiting my life." The man pointed to the meteoric alloy dummy in the ring and proclaimed, "Therefore, I want to see whose sword skill is the better!"

t_ch: 「若是我占得上风，请云骑将军赏我个一官半职！」一位银发将军坐在台下，笑眯眯地看见这擂官，懒洋洋地说着「无妨」。
t_en: "If I were proven to be the better, may the Cloud Knight General confer unto me an official title!" A silver-haired general seating beneath the ring smiled as he looked at this ringmaster, lazily proclaiming "That can be arranged."

t_ch: 景元将军！
t_en: General Jing Yuan!

t_ch: （太长了，下次再听吧）
t_en: (This story is taking too long. I'll listen to it another time.)

t_ch: 无论是云骑新兵、还是入伍多年的老兵，对着这块异星金属打造的靶人都无可奈何——强壮的士兵能一剑斩出剑痕，而稍弱的士兵甚至留不下痕迹。
t_en: None was a match before this target dummy forged from alien meteoric alloy, be it newbie Cloud Knight or even the most experienced veterans. The stronger soldiers could leave a mark on the dummy, but the weaker ones could leave nary a scratch.

t_ch: 这彪形大汉对云骑士兵的剑术连连摇头，再次开口「这百人试剑结束，竟无一人伤得了这小小靶人，看我一剑——」
t_en: This burly fellow kept shaking his head at the Cloud Knights Soldier's swordsmanship, and spoke again: "Thus ends the hundred-men sword trial. To think none could harm this meager dummy... Watch my blade!"

t_ch: 好家伙，这疤面大汉拿起云骑军的钝剑，瞬时间一道寒光闪过，这陨铁靶人被他生生地一分为二了。校兵场上欢呼声雷动，很多新兵第一次见到这样的场面。
t_en: 'twas a sight to behold. This scarred man took up a Cloud Knight's blunt sword, and with a silvery flash, this meteoric alloy dummy was instantly cleaved in two. The parade square roared with cheers, for it was the first time many recruits had seen such a feat.

t_ch: 「这校兵场内如果没有高人打擂，我可不仅要这一官半职，还得要抹粉戴花、游街示众了！哈哈哈……」
t_en: "If there are no more skilled challengers in this parade square, I would ask for not just an official title, but to be wreathed in flowers and paraded through the streets for all to see! Hahaha..."

t_ch: 云骑军一名小队长仰面向台上看了看，未等这疤面剑客说完，双脚点地，飞身蹿上擂台，朝着靶人就是一剑，那两半靶人应声变成了碎块。
t_en: A Cloud Knights captain eyed the ring, and before the scarred swordsman could finish his sentence, the captain pushed himself off the ground with his feet, sprang up to the ring, and flashed his sword at the broken dummy, turning the dummy into pieces.

t_ch: 这一剑着实让疤面剑客脸色一青，他并未想到云骑军中有此等高手，但他佯装镇定「这位队长确实有些水平……」
t_en: The scarred swordsman's face paled at that single strike. He had not expected such talent among the Cloud Knights, but he feigned composure: "This captain does have some skills..."

t_ch: 「但是没有讨教到『无罅飞光』，我还不能从这擂台上撤下，哪位好汉是『无罅飞光』，快来让本大爷长长见识。」
t_en: "But I can't leave this ring without having learned a thing or two from the Transcendent Flash. Who among you is the Transcendent Flash? Hurry up and let yours truly witness your strength."

t_ch: 士兵们议论纷纷，谁也没去阻止他。新兵们虽然对这人赖在擂台不下心生不满，却也想知道「无罅飞光」的剑技究竟如何。
t_en: The soldiers murmured among themselves. No one confronted him. The recruits were unhappy about this man's shamelessness, but they also wished to know the power of the Transcendent Flash.

t_ch: 此时，一名白发剑士缓缓登上擂台，整个校兵场瞬时安静了下来——就像一缕孤高的月光拂过，众人不禁屏息凝神。
t_en: Right then, a silver-haired soldier steadily climbed onto the ring, and the entire parade square immediately fell silent — as if beholding a lofty moonlight streak whisking past, everyone held their breaths.

t_ch: 「我的剑，谁要看，看便是。」
t_en: "Those who wish to see my sword, come and see it."

t_ch: 无罅飞光！
t_en: The Transcendent Flash!

t_ch: （太长了，下次再听吧）
t_en: (This story is taking too long. I'll listen to it another time.)

t_ch: 可是这靶人已经变成了碎块，怎么才能看出这一剑的威力呢？白发剑士，取出好友相赠的宝剑，朝着校场上空挥了一击。
t_en: But this dummy has been shredded into pieces. How would one be able to judge the strength of a blade? The ivory-haired soldier unsheathed the prized sword gifted by a friend and swung it at the air.

t_ch: 在那一霎，整个校场似乎阴阳倒错、晨昏颠倒。在众人回过神时，「无罅飞光」已经收剑入鞘，失重一般的眩晕感却仍在持续。
t_en: In an instant, it was as if day turned into night over the parade square, and time itself had reverted. When everyone came to their senses, the Transcendent Flash had already sheathed the blade, but the dizzy feeling of vertigo persisted.

t_ch: 校兵场上所有人不禁在心中浮现出一个共同的想法——这一剑削去了时间。
t_en: Everyone in the parade square was thinking of the exact same thing — this strike had sliced through time itself.

t_ch: 静静缓缓流动的时间，原本如同流水一般持续不断，而「抽刀断水」之事更应该是无稽之谈…但这一剑，却实实在在地削去了时间。
t_en: Time, flowing slowly and silently, is supposed to trickle steadily like water, and it should have been impossible to deliver a "Water-Severing Strike"... but this slash had indeed pierced time itself.

t_ch: 只此一剑落下，擂台上的胜负已经无关紧要了。
t_en: With this decisive strike, the result in the ring had become inconsequential.

t_ch: 先生的话本该更新了吧？
t_en: It's time for you to update your story, isn't it?

t_ch: 是啊，在下的不少评书话本也要跟着更新了…以前那些「建木断绝数千年，至今再未复生」之类的桥段，在下全都要改了。
t_en: Yes, many of my stories have to be updated soon... I have to amend those parts that talk about how the Ambrosial Arbor has never resurrected for millennia.

t_ch: 唉…这种查缺补漏的工作真的是大工程…要一不小心说走了嘴，底下观众可真不给钱！
t_en: Alas... it's a very tedious process to spot and rectify all these errors in the stories... If I say anything wrong, my audience seriously won't tip me!

t_ch: 说来也是有趣，这建木竟然以这样的方式，对在下的工作造成了显著的影响…这估计寿瘟祸祖本尊都想不到吧，哈哈。
t_en: Who would have thought the Ambrosial Arbor would significantly affect my work in this way... I bet even the Plagues Author couldn't have expected this, haha.

t_ch: 欢迎光临不夜侯！客人几位，想要喝点儿什么茶？我是小店的主人梦茗，有什么吩咐您随时告诉我。
t_en: Welcome to the Sleepless Earl! How many of you are there? Which tea would you like today? My name's Mengming, and this is my teahouse. Let me know if there's anything I can do.

t_ch: 有什么推荐？
t_en: Any recommendations?

t_ch: 今日惠风和畅，很适合试试应季新芽的「狩原毛峰」，这茶我们店里从来不敢怠慢，要求上午采，下午制；下午采，当夜制…保证客人喝到最新鲜的。
t_en: There's a gentle breeze today, so it's a good time to try our fresh Hunter Maofeng which is in season. We never neglect this tea in our shop, making sure we pick fresh leaves every morning for the afternoon's brew, and afternoon leaves for the evening... ensuring you only drink the freshest.

t_ch: 不过按照茶馆的规矩，还得先问问客官的口味，您是喜欢喝茶汤浓的，还是喜欢喝一些清淡的呢？
t_en: But in this teahouse, we like to ask the customer's preference. Do you like your tea thick and strong, or mild?

t_ch: 我喜欢喝浓的。
t_en: I like it strong.

t_ch: 那我推荐您试试「仙舟红茶」，虽说茶水浓淡与个人口味和感受有关，但要是浓苦的茶，咱们店也是端不出来的。
t_en: Ah, then may I suggest the Xianzhou Black Tea? The strength of the tea depends on personal tastes and sensations, but we refuse to serve tea that's too strong and bitter.

t_ch: 喝茶享受，可不是来找罪受的，而客人喜欢稍浓一些的茶汤，这「仙舟红茶」汤色红，入口微涩，对您来说是再合适不过的。
t_en: Drinking tea is for pleasure and not a torment. Since you like it rather strong, then this Xianzhou Black Tea with its red color and slightly astringent taste should suit you perfectly.

t_ch: 我喜欢喝清淡的。
t_en: I like it mild.

t_ch: 那我推荐您试试「丹鼎素针」，用盖碗泡着喝，沸水冲泡，快速出汤，这茶汤不浓不苦，鲜香醇厚正正好。
t_en: Then I recommend you try our Vegetations in the Alchemy Cauldron variety. Brew it quickly in a covered cup with boiling water and you'll soon enjoy some tea that's neither thick nor bitter. The flavor is just right.

t_ch: 俗话说，清水出芙蓉，天然去雕饰。好喝的淡茶呢，并非寡淡无味，而是淡而不薄，客人来两壶品品就明白了。
t_en: As the saying goes, the freshwater hibiscus is Nature's perfect sculpture. A good mild tea is not bland and tasteless, but mild and not thin. Try a couple of pots and you'll know what I mean.

t_ch: 我的口味不挑剔。
t_en: I'm not fussy at all.

t_ch: 来小店里最挑剔的客人也老爱说这句话。
t_en: That's what our fussiest customers are always saying.

t_ch: 那咱们就来品品新茶，试试口味。这应季新芽的「狩原毛峰」初展肥壮嫩芽，入杯冲泡，滋味醇甘，香气如兰。
t_en: Okay, let's try sampling a new tea then. This season's new buds of Hunter Maofeng have begun to ripen up. Brew it for a mellow sweet flavor and rich floral bouquet.

t_ch: 抱歉，我不是来喝茶的。
t_en: Sorry, I'm not here for tea.

t_ch: 明白了，要是想来不夜侯听人说书，现在可还不是时候，先生他呀还没有准备好。要不，您点壶茶，在店里坐一会儿怎么样呢？
t_en: I see. If you've come to the Sleepless Earl to hear some stories, you're too early — Xiyan xiansheng isn't ready yet. How about a pot of tea to while away the time?

t_ch: 顺带一提，客人如果着急公干商谈的话，现在店里也有提供瓶装带走的冷萃饮品。
t_en: By the way, if you're on business and in a hurry, we stock a range of cold bottled beverages for you to take away.

t_ch: 小小老板娘，能说会道。
t_en: You've really got the gift of the gab.

t_ch: 哪里哪里，我也是才学着做生意，说话笨，有什么不合适的地方还望客人多多包容，这茶楼里每一位客人都是我的老师，您也不例外呀。
t_en: You're too kind. I just started studying commerce and am not good at talking shop. Please forgive me if I ever behaved inappropriately. Every customer here is my teacher, and you are no exception.

t_ch: 茶汤的讲究、饮茶的规矩…样样都是学问，还望老师不吝赐教。
t_en: Brewing tea, rules of the tea ceremony... it's all knowledge. Please be generous with your tips and tutelage.

t_ch: 老板娘不会在欺负我不懂茶吧？
t_en: You won't bully me for not understanding anything about tea, right?

t_ch: 岂敢岂敢，我这小店从父母那里继承而来，来店喝茶的客人大多是行家里手，梦茗可没有什么能够卖弄之处，还得向客人老师们多多取经。
t_en: You flatter me! I inherited this store from my parents, and most of our customers are experts. I don't have any particular skills to show off. Rather, there's much I can learn from our guests here.

t_ch: 茶汤的讲究、饮茶的规矩…样样都是学问，还望老师不吝赐教。
t_en: Brewing tea, rules of the tea ceremony... it's all knowledge. Please be generous with your tips and tutelage.

t_ch: 客人要是想聊，还得到里面坐坐…嗯，客人难道还有别的事儿要问梦茗吗？
t_en: If a customer wants to chat, they have to sit inside... well, is there anything else that you'd like to ask me?

t_ch: 就简单地再聊两句。
t_en: Just a couple of more things.

t_ch: 哎，客人这是看准了小店现在不忙。那行，客官有什么不清楚的礼节执贽，都可以问问我，我也向您讨教讨教这做生意的本事。
t_en: Ah, you spotted that we're rather quiet right now. Alright, if there are any aspects of etiquette that you don't understand, ask me, and I'll pick your brains about doing business.

t_ch: 饮茶都有些什么讲究啊？
t_en: What are the rules in tea appreciation?

t_ch: 要是这茶饮讲究全都讲起来未必有点太长，我们就讲「选茗」、「蓄水」这两个规矩吧。
t_en: It would take forever to explain all the rules. I'll just talk about tea selection and water retention for now.

t_ch: 说到「选茗」，就拿我们店的狩原毛峰举例吧，不夜侯只选采其中最珍贵的凤舌品种，芽尖如峰，银毫披身。
t_en: Speaking of tea selection, take our Hunter Maofeng tea as an example. The Sleepless Earl only picks the most precious buds, usually long, pointed, and covered in fine hairs.

t_ch: 再说这「蓄水」，茶客们常说「乳泉石池之水」最佳，但这鳞渊境的波月古海之水才是真正的善水，炼丹煮茶，没有比它更好的了。
t_en: As for water retention, tea merchants always say that mineral water is the best — but the clear and ancient water in Scalegorge Waterscape is the best. There's no better water for tea and alchemy than this.

t_ch: 我也是有样学样，说得不对的地方，还请客人多多批评。
t_en: I'm also only learning and invite you to call me out if anything I've said is wrong.

t_ch: 怎么想到在这里开一家茶馆的？
t_en: What made you want to open up a teahouse here?

t_ch: 客人误会了，这家小店是家父开的，不夜侯在宣夜大街上也有四百余载啦！
t_en: My parents opened this teahouse, not me. The Sleepless Earl has been on Starwatcher Avenue for over four hundred years!

t_ch: 家父当时想着临近港口，过路的行人旅客需要歇脚休息。这茶水生意肯定能红红火火，得各位客官照顾，才有了今日之成就。
t_en: Dad wanted to be near to the port, with all the tired and thirsty people nearby. It was always sure to be a booming business, and we owe all our successes to the kindness of our customers.

t_ch: 小老板娘做生意有模有样的。
t_en: You are the model of a good young business owner.

t_ch: 我…我不小了！这本就是该当家作主的年龄了。虽然世人常道「狐人天生会行商」，但事实上是这都是磨砺出来的。
t_en: I'm... not young! I'm just at the right age to take over the family business. People may always say we foxians are naturals when it comes to business, but really it's all practice.

t_ch: 家父说是要考验考验我的本事，便留下一座茶楼，和家母四处云游去了。这做生意的门道，我也还远远没到登堂入室的程度。
t_en: Dad said that he wanted to test me, so he left me the teahouse and wandered off traveling with mom. I'm still a long way off from being proficient in doing business.

t_ch: 现在许多问题我还是得请教西衍先生，客人要是也有心相助的话，请不吝赐教。
t_en: Even now, I still have to ask Mr. Xiyan lots of questions. If you're willing to support me, please be generous with your criticism.

t_ch: 没有什么想问的了。
t_en: I don't have any other questions.

t_ch: 无妨，等客人得闲的时候，再来光顾。
t_en: It's okay. Come back when you've got more time to spare.

t_ch: 下次有机会再说吧。
t_en: Let us talk next time.

t_ch: 无妨，等客人得闲的时候，再来光顾。
t_en: It's okay. Come back when you've got more time to spare.

t_ch: 一回生二回熟，客人再度光临不夜侯！您今天想要喝点什么？
t_en: It's you again, welcome back to the Sleepless Earl! What can I get for you?

t_ch: 啊…我明白了，客人这是还有别的事儿要问梦茗，请讲吧。
t_en: Ahhh... I see. You've got more questions for me, do you? Go on.

t_ch: 我想来杯茶。
t_en: I'd like a cup of tea.

t_ch: 建木危机闹得可真大……
t_en: The Ambrosial Arbor Crisis has blown up big time...

t_ch: 是啊，先前建木生发之时，我就在想，以前父母总跟我说，要化危机为机遇。既然这建木生发是一场危机，那必然也是能转化成机遇的吧？
t_en: Totally. Before the Ambrosial Arbor's growth happened, I had been thinking about my parents, who always told me to turn a crisis into an opportunity. Since the Ambrosial Arbor's new growth was a crisis, I thought I would be able to turn it into an opportunity.

t_ch: 如果能将它变成机遇，不就可以让茶楼经营得更好了吗？所以，我就去找西衍先生商量这件事了。
t_en: If I succeeded, it could be a great boost to my tea house's business. So I went to Mr. Xiyan to discuss it.

t_ch: 结果西衍先生却说「绝对不应当惦记着用这种事情获利」，然后教育了我一顿……
t_en: In the end, Mr. Xiyan told me off and said that I shouldn't have thought of profiting from the crisis...

t_ch: 我还没想清楚该如何应对这危机，转眼之间，建木的危机又平息了。唉，世事瞬息万变，教人如何反应得过来。经商之道可真难啊…看来我还有很长的路要走呢。
t_en: And the crisis ended in the blink of an eye before I could come up with a plan to make use of it. *sigh* Things are ever-changing in this world, and it teaches us to be adaptive to changes. Running a business is tough, and I still have a long way to go.

t_ch: 这位朋友，你那个星舰长得挺别致啊。怎么样？需要新船员吗？
t_en: My friend, that starship you've got there, it's gorgeous. How about it? Fancy a new crew member?

t_ch: 暂时不需要…
t_en: Not at the moment...

t_ch: 别这么说嘛。站在你面前的可是特级星槎驾驶员、保险定损甲级专家、乙级护卫舰维修师、特级厨师、星际和平公司C级销售员、甲级玉兆修理工——旭霞！
t_en: Don't be so hasty. Standing before you is an S-ranked starskiff pilot, A-ranked damage assessment expert, B-ranked ship mechanic, S-Ranked chef, C-ranked IPC sales associate, and A-ranked jade abacus fixer: Xuxia!

t_ch: 你是？
t_en: And you are?

t_ch: 哼哼，说出来吓死你。站在你面前的可是特级星槎驾驶员、保险定损甲级专家、乙级护卫舰维修师、特级厨师、星际和平公司C级销售员、甲级玉兆修理工——旭霞！
t_en: Heheh, you'll be shivering in your boots when I announce my accolades. Standing before you is an S-ranked starskiff pilot, A-ranked damage assessment expert, B-ranked ship mechanic, S-Ranked chef, C-ranked IPC sales associate, and A-ranked jade abacus fixer: Xuxia!

t_ch: 我们的车上装不下那么多人…
t_en: We can't fit that many people in our ride...

t_ch: 什么啊！这都是我旭霞一个人！你去全银河打听打听，「仙舟万事通」说的就是我旭霞。
t_en: Wha-! It's all just one person! Xuxia! Me! Go ask around the cosmos! Everyone knows that "The Multifaceted" is me!

t_ch: 这个事，你得问列车长。
t_en: You'll have to ask the conductor about this.

t_ch: 什么？你不是管事儿的吗，虽然我一看就知道。那行，我想想办法，找个机会问问你说的列车长。
t_en: What? Aren't you in charge around here? It was rhetorical anyway. Fine, I'll think of something and ask that conductor of yours when an opportunity arises.

t_ch: 列车欢迎不同世界的乘客。
t_en: The Express welcomes passengers from any world.

t_ch: 乘客？你们那儿没有我能做的工作吗，那就算了，我对你们这艘星舰不感兴趣了。
t_en: Passenger? Have you got nothing around here that I can do? Forget it then. I'm not interested in this starship of yours any longer.

t_ch: …哎，不为难你了。仔细想想，我现在也没有急着要立刻出发。
t_en: ...*sigh* I won't impose then. Come to think of it, I'm not in a rush to leave right now anyway.

t_ch: 你现在在干什么？
t_en: What are you doing now?

t_ch: 我在休息，顺便等待下一个远行的机会。
t_en: I'm taking a break, and waiting for a chance for a voyage while I'm at it.

t_ch: 仙舟是不允许长生种的永久迁徙舟外的，好像是生态灾难之类的说法，我也不懂…所以，想要出门远行，就必须找到一份可以合法外驻的工作。
t_en: Xianzhou prohibits long-life species from dwelling beyond the ship. Something about ecological disasters, I'm not too sure either... So if you want to embark on a voyage, you need to find interstellar work.

t_ch: 为了实现这个目标，我考了特别多的职业资格证，刚才我提到的只不过是九牛一毛。
t_en: To reach that goal, I took plenty of professional qualification certifications. The vocations I just mentioned were some of many.

t_ch: 来，这个送给你，这是我在外面旅行时发现的东西。不过，你可能见过很多类似的东西了吧。
t_en: I'll give you this: Something I found while traveling outside. Though, you must've seen plenty of these.

t_ch: 哎，在规划好下一次的远行之前，我都会暂时留在仙舟，要是想聊天的话，随时欢迎。
t_en: *sigh* Before the next voyage, I'll be hanging around on the Xianzhou. You're welcome to chat anytime you want.

t_ch: 想再多问问你。
t_en: I want to ask you about something else.

t_ch: 好啊，正好我蛮闲的。你想知道什么？
t_en: Sure, I'm bored as it is. What do you wish to know?

t_ch: 你去过很多地方吗？
t_en: Have you been to many places?

t_ch: 坦白说，我觉得和那些短生种的旅行家比起来，我去过的地方绝对谈不上特别多。毕竟长生种离开仙舟并不是很容易。
t_en: Frankly speaking, compared to travelers with short lifespans, I don't think I've been to that many places. It's hard enough for long-life species to leave Xianzhou as it is.

t_ch: 不过毕竟我的年纪也比短生种长，要是我说自己去过的地方特别少，也显得有点儿不够真诚。
t_en: But seeing that I've lived longer than any of the short-life species, it'd be a little patronizing to say I haven't been to many places.

t_ch: 这些年，我考取了很多职业资格证，就是为了能够在任何船队的任何岗位有空缺时挤进去。
t_en: Over the years, I've taken plenty of professional certifications to fill up any vacancies a crew might have.

t_ch: 让我想想看，这些年我跟过…体育代表团、学术访问团、曲艺团、马戏团、外交使团…还有……
t_en: Let's see, over the years I've followed... sports representative associations, academician visitations, folk troupes, circuses, diplomatic missions... and...

t_ch: 反物质军团。
t_en: The Antimatter Legion.

t_ch: 你别以为我没听说过，他们全都是些祸害银河的坏东西。
t_en: Don't assume I don't know about them. They're all bad eggs who bring doom to the cosmos.

t_ch: 盗宝团。
t_en: Space pirates.

t_ch: 那是什么团啦！听起来就不是什么好鸟！我可是守法公民，从没做过任何非法的事情。
t_en: What sort of group is that? That doesn't sound like anything nice! I'm a law-abiding citizen who has never harmed a fly.

t_ch: （适当地沉默）
t_en: (Deliberate silence)

t_ch: …合唱团！我可想起来了，我唱的是中音的位置。
t_en: ... Choirs! I remember now, I sang alto.

t_ch: 简而言之，我会的本事越多，能去的地方就越多，现在我已经去过很多地方了，但是这还不够，我还想去更多地方。
t_en: Long story short, the more I knew, the more I could travel. And now I've been to many places, but that's not enough. I want to travel even more.

t_ch: 留在仙舟遇到什么不顺利了吗？
t_en: Are things not going well for you on Xianzhou?

t_ch: 谈不上不顺利吧。我从小就是坐不住的性格，十二岁时拿到「狐人大鼓一级演员」资格，十八岁成为「特级厨师」。
t_en: I wouldn't say that. Since young, I've always had a hard time sticking around. I was a certified "Foxian Grade 1 Grand Drum Actor" when I was twelve, and then an "S-Ranked Chef" at eighteen.

t_ch: 对仙舟大部分人来说，这张资格证就决定了至少四百年的生活。可万一我不喜欢这工作了呢，你能想象吗？不喜欢的工作做个四百年。
t_en: To most Xianzhou natives, this certification grants you at least four centuries of livelihood. But what if I didn't like the job? Can you imagine? Slogging away at a gig you hated for four hundred years?

t_ch: 我无法忍受，我绝对无法忍受。
t_en: I can't, and I never will.

t_ch: 虽然说狐人可以活个几百年，但是在仙舟这样死气沉沉的环境中，活一年、十年、一百年、一万年，有任何区别吗？反正每天都一模一样。
t_en: Foxians live for centuries, but living for one, ten, a hundred, ten thousand years, in such a drab place like Xianzhou... what's the point? It's all the same mundane monotony day in, day out.

t_ch: 所以我才无论如何都要到外面的世界去旅行。在充满变数和刺激的生活中，即使只有一昼夜的生命，也好过一亿个昼夜。
t_en: That's why I had to head out to travel, no matter what it took. In a life full of unknowns and thrills, one day of that beats a lifetime of such predictable doldrums.

t_ch: 回到仙舟一般做些什么？
t_en: What do you normally do back on Xianzhou?

t_ch: 跟家人聚一下，再拜访一下朋友…你听说过仙舟的「纸鸢」吗，我就像是飞上天的燕子，而这些我惦记的「家人朋友」就是那根线。
t_en: Hang out with family, visit friends... Have you heard of Xianzhou's paper kites? I am like a swallow that soars to the skies, and my friends and family are the string that anchors me.

t_ch: 我会把找来的宝贝卖给「赎珠阁」的瀚典掌柜，我们俩私交甚好，他对我不遮不掩，能当着面指出我高价买到的宝石是「远古玻璃球」。
t_en: I will sell the treasures I bring back to Pawnbroker Handian at the Jeweler's Pagoda. We get along well, and he never hustles me either — he'll tell me straight to the face that the priceless gem I bought is an "ancient glass ball."

t_ch: 哈哈哈，这种安心感的感觉，除了回到仙舟，在哪里也都找不到。
t_en: Hahaha, that sense of homely comfort, you can't find it anywhere else but Xianzhou.

t_ch: 再接下来，就是在仙舟上到处逛逛，谋划下次旅行的机会吧。
t_en: Next up, it's time to walk around Xianzhou and plot my next chance at a voyage.

t_ch: 无意冒犯，我可能比你多活数倍，要是生活不如你精彩，这不是很可笑吗？我要活得比你精彩百倍！
t_en: I didn't mean to offend. I've probably lived much longer than you — it would be a travesty if my life was that less exciting than yours! It's almost imperative that I lead a more interesting life!

t_ch: 没什么想问的了。
t_en: That's all the questions I have.

t_ch: 嗯嗯。如果有什么适合我的外驻工作，记得推荐给我哦。
t_en: All right then. Don't forget to drop my name if you find any interstellar work suitable for me.

t_ch: 没什么事，我先走了。
t_en: Nothing. Sorry, I have to go.

t_ch: 嗯嗯。如果有什么适合我的外驻工作，记得推荐给我哦。
t_en: All right then. Don't forget to drop my name if you find any interstellar work suitable for me.

t_ch: 好巧啊，又见面了，看来我们颇有缘分呐。
t_en: What a coincidence; we meet again. It looks like our fates might be intertwined.

t_ch: 今天我还去了司辰宫一趟，问了问熟人有没有外派工作需要人手，等来的只有「近期没有，再等等。」
t_en: I even went down to the Palace of Astrum today and asked an associate there if there were any interstellar vacancies. All I got was "not recently, wait a while longer."

t_ch: 你要是知道什么有什么适合我的外驻工作，千万别忘了我呀。对了，你找我有什么事情吗？
t_en: Don't forget to drop my name if you find any interstellar work suitable for me. Oh right, you wanted to find me for something?

t_ch: 建木生发又静止，真是神奇。
t_en: The Ambrosial Arbor grew then stopped. It's a miracle.

t_ch: 看过了，其实感觉没啥意思…一是那边戒严，不让靠近。二是这东西本身也没什么看头。
t_en: Having seen the tree, I'm not too impressed. Firstly, the place is heavily guarded, and people can't get too close to it. Secondly, there's nothing special about it.

t_ch: 你见过帕忽热里克的铬树林吗？参天大树似的铬柱，像真正的树林一样遮天蔽日。而且那可是真正的自然造物，真正的鬼斧神工！而不是什么莫名其妙的星神遗产。
t_en: Have you been to the chromium forest in Pakhurik? Towering chromium pillars that resemble massive trees block out sunlight like the canopy of a real forest. That forest is a fantastic creation of nature, a true miracle of the world! Instead of some mysterious legacy of an Aeon!

t_ch: 强烈推荐你有机会时去看看，绝对比这个建木壮丽千倍万倍——还不会害人。
t_en: I strongly recommend that you take a trip there. It's definitely much more magnificent than the Ambrosial Arbor, and it isn't dangerous.

t_ch: 鱼尾已经完全失去胶质了，即使再选用宁蜇去熬煮勾兑，也只是徒增烦恼……
t_en: The fish tail has completely lost its chewiness. Even if you stew it with jellyfish to blend the texture, it will only add to your troubles...

t_ch: 算了算了，我居然还在认真回想这饭菜口味，要是再想下去，我就得当众呕吐出来了。
t_en: Forget it. To think I even seriously reminisced on this dish's taste. I think I'd throw up on the spot if I gave it any further thought.

t_ch: 话不用说得这么难听吧？
t_en: That's a bit harsh, isn't it?

t_ch: 这就难听了？我还有更难听的。
t_en: THAT'S harsh? I've got even worse comments.

t_ch: 星槎海简直就是罗浮仙舟上的美食洼地，全银河失业的厨师都来这里找到了工作。
t_en: Starskiff Haven is where the art of cooking goes to die on the Xianzhou Luofu. All the unemployed chefs across the cosmos have found jobs here.

t_ch: 到底是哪一家不好吃啊？
t_en: Which restaurant tastes bad, exactly?

t_ch: 哪一家不好吃？…笑死了。
t_en: Which one's bad? ...You're funny.

t_ch: 每一家都难吃。
t_en: All of them are bad.

t_ch: 我建议星槎海的所有厨师聚在一起办一个厨王争霸比赛，输了的一律不许开店。
t_en: I'd suggest for all the chefs in Starskiff Haven to compete in a culinary showdown. Those who lose must close their restaurants.

t_ch: 那个侥幸赢了的，必须再跟我来比一场，我亲自把这里的厨师一个不剩地赶出去。
t_en: The ones who win through sheer luck will have to pit their skills against me. I will personally drive all the chefs here out.

t_ch: 你也是厨师吗？
t_en: You're a chef too?

t_ch: 也算是吧，不过今天我只是普通的食客。
t_en: Sort of. Though I'm just a regular customer here today.

t_ch: 我不太愿意在这里说自己是一个厨师。毕竟，是个人、会烧水就能在星槎海做厨师。
t_en: I don't really like telling people I'm a chef here. I mean, you can get a cooking job at Starskiff Haven as long as you have a pulse and know how to boil water.

t_ch: 我这人自怜自爱，因此必须跟他们划清界限，希望你理解。
t_en: I both pity and love my passion, which is why I must draw the line with these amateurs. I hope you understand.

t_ch: 你是美食家吗？
t_en: Are you a gastronome?

t_ch: 你误会了，我只是个偶尔烧菜的食客罢了，今天是专程来星槎海涨学问来的。
t_en: You misunderstand. I am but a foodie who sometimes cooks. I have specially come here today to widen my culinary horizons on the Starskiff Haven.

t_ch: 正常人怎么都想不出来，要怎么做，要加什么东西，才能这么难吃。所以，他们肯定有什么秘密调料我还没有学会。
t_en: Ordinary folks could never figure out how to make such terrible dishes even if they tried. Therefore, they must have some secret condiment that I have yet to learn.

t_ch: …不是咸不是淡不是辣不是甜，是一种难吃的极意，是凡人到达不了的境界。
t_en: ...It's not salty, nor bland, nor spicy, nor sweet — it is the epitome of "yuck." It is not a culinary state that mortals can attain.

t_ch: 我叫作高唐，在仙舟开有一家小小的私房菜馆，名曰「至味盛苑」。虽然一座难求，但是比起星槎海，小店依然保有对顾客的基本尊重。
t_en: I am Gao Tang, and I own a private kitchen in Xianzhou called the Garden of Gourmet. It may be a hole-in-the-wall restaurant, but compared to the rest of the Starskiff Haven, my humble restaurant respects all its customers.

t_ch: 如果你现在肚子稍微有些饿的感受，我建议你立刻另寻他处，以免被饥饿感绑架，被迫在这里的餐厅受苦。毕竟，这实在是不值得。
t_en: If your tummy is even the least bit hungry, I suggest you find another place this instant, or you will be hostage to your hunger, and you will be forced into suffering in this place. It is simply not worth it.

t_ch: 是不是你的标准太高了？
t_en: Could your standards be too high?

t_ch: 你误会了，我已经记不得我有多少次在星槎海因为难吃而痛苦愤怒。这是因为我的标准太高了吗？不对，这是一种本源的、生理的排斥。
t_en: You are mistaken. I have long forgotten the number of times I've lost my temper from the terrible food at Starskiff Haven. Is it because my standards are high? Nay, it is a raw, visceral reaction to their food.

t_ch: 你试试就明白了——啊不对，为了你着想，你还是不要试了。
t_en: You'll understand once you try... ah, that's not right. It's best if you don't try.

t_ch: 难道说你做得就很好吃了？
t_en: What, so the food you make is tasty?

t_ch: 确实，我做得很好吃。
t_en: Naturally. My dishes taste great.

t_ch: 我常常在担忧，在烦恼——我做得这么好吃，对星槎海的厨师真的公平吗？他们不会因为我的存在而惭愧吗？
t_en: I've always struggled with this notion: Is it really fair that my food tastes heavenly compared to Starskiff Haven's chefs? Would they not be ashamed in light of my existence?

t_ch: 不好意思，又自顾自说了这么多。千言万语不如你直接去我的店里尝尝，从现在开始预约，估计两年后就能吃到了。
t_en: I'm sorry. I'm tooting my own horn again. Instead of throwing words at you, why not pop over to my restaurant for a taste? If you make a reservation now, you'll get a table in about two years.

t_ch: 我看你对美食也有一定的兴趣，有什么想问的，随时欢迎。
t_en: I see you have some interest in the arts of culinary persuasion as well. You're welcome to ask me anything.

t_ch: 想和你聊聊。
t_en: I want to have a chat with you.

t_ch: 哦，尽管问，只有谈及美食，我还算是颇有心得的。
t_en: Oh, ask away. I have much to share, as long as it's about food.

t_ch: 说真的，星槎海没有美食吗？
t_en: Is it true? That there's no good food in the Starskiff Haven?

t_ch: 严格来说，星槎海的食物足以果腹，但还谈不上是什么美食。
t_en: Strictly speaking, Starskiff Haven's food is enough to sate your hunger. Whether it tastes good is another matter.

t_ch: 没有严格挑选的食材，没有充分思考的处理，这样随随便便做出来的东西我是不会认可的。
t_en: Without stringent selection nor prudent handling of ingredients, I refuse to accept food that has been made haphazardly.

t_ch: 不过你放心，星槎海这里的食物不能代表仙舟美食，在仙舟上仍然能找到对美食有所追求的餐厅。
t_en: But don't worry. Starskiff Haven's food does not represent Xianzhou's culinary landscape. You can still find restaurants dedicated to the craft here.

t_ch: 别的不说，你要是吃过「至味盛苑」的菜品，估计你对这些劣质的食物也没有什么兴趣了。
t_en: If nothing else, once you've tried the dishes at the Garden of Gourmet, I'm sure you will lose interest in this subpar fare.

t_ch: 「至味盛苑」做什么样的菜？
t_en: What kind of food does the Garden of Gourmet serve?

t_ch: 最近餐厅在做翔鲟公，这鱼五十年一跃，是「安河五君」中最重要的食材。
t_en: The restaurant's been making sturgeons recently. This type of fish returns once every fifty years, and is a key ingredient in "Five Lords of Anhe River."

t_ch: 「至味盛苑」就只取其尾部最脆的三根肉骨，吊煮百草高汤沃沐，再取鲜羊凝油化浆，油煎慢炸，直至肉骨焦酥。
t_en: In the Garden of Gourmet, we extract the three crunchiest bones from the tail, boil them in a broth of myriad herbs, then grill them using fresh goat's fat paste. At the final stage, we slow fry them till the meaty bones are caramelized and crisp.

t_ch: 这个过程里，有任何一步不满意，我都不会上菜。这也是为什么「至味盛苑」没有菜单，一天只能接待一桌客人。
t_en: Any step that's not up to snuff in this process, and I'll dump the dish entirely. This is also why the Garden of Gourmet has no menu — we only serve one table a day.

t_ch: 食不厌精，脍不厌细。仙舟上人活千年，饮食之道上必须这样讲究。
t_en: No such thing as food too fine, or meat too minced. Those of Xianzhou have lived for a thousand years, we must be meticulous with what goes in our mouths.

t_ch: 你谈到料理的时候好尖锐。
t_en: You speak with such passion when discussing cuisine.

t_ch: 人在一定程度上是被食物塑造的。所以，你能接受自己被粗制滥造吗？
t_en: We are all, in some way, shaped by our food. You wouldn't want to be made shoddily, would you?

t_ch: 不能接受。
t_en: I wouldn't.

t_ch: 没错。
t_en: I thought so.

t_ch: 我无所谓。
t_en: I'm fine either way.

t_ch: 无所谓？
t_en: Either way?

t_ch: 我早就已经是烂人了。
t_en: I'm not exactly a role model to begin with.

t_ch: 我有点担心你。
t_en: I worry for you.

t_ch: 我看你是化外民，一生能吃的餐食也是有限的，就更得谨慎选择了吧。
t_en: I notice you are an Outworlder. Your food tapestry must be limited, so you must make wiser choices.

t_ch: 对食物要求严格，就是在对「人之所以为人」严格。
t_en: To be strict with what you eat is to be strict with the "reason why we are people."

t_ch: 其他的，下次再问你吧。
t_en: I'll ask you about something else next time.

t_ch: 那有缘再见。
t_en: May we meet again.

t_ch: 下次有机会再说吧。
t_en: Let us talk next time.

t_ch: 那有缘再见。
t_en: May we meet again.

t_ch: 哎，不会做就酱爆的就不要写酱爆了，直接写清炒什么都行。
t_en: *sigh* Don't say it's "bursting with juices" if you can't cook it this way. Just write "stir-fried" or something.

t_ch: 哎呀，我看这星槎海也算是块风水宝地，味觉失灵的厨师都在这里找到了新的人生。
t_en: Hmm, the way I see Starskiff Haven, it's a sort of promised land. Even chefs who've lost their taste buds can find new beginnings here.

t_ch: {F#小妹妹}{M#小兄弟}又碰见你了。想吃点什么，记得去「至味盛苑」预定一桌吧，别在星槎海浪费时间了。你找我是有什么事儿吗？
t_en: {F#Young lady}{M#Young man}, fancy bumping into you again. What'll you have? Don't forget to make a reservation at the Garden of Gourmet, and stop wasting your time at Starskiff Haven. What can I do for you?

t_ch: 建木的事，你应该也知道吧？
t_en: I suppose you know what has happened to the Ambrosial Arbor?

t_ch: 没有冒犯你的意思，但我对这个话题没什么兴趣。它长起来能怎么样呢，它停止生长了又能怎么样呢？吃饭才是人生的头等大事啊。
t_en: No offense, but I'm not interested in this topic. So what if it grows or not? Filling up my stomach is at the top of my priority list.

t_ch: 对所有吃饭和做饭以外的话题，我都没什么兴趣，咱们还是聊聊美食吧。
t_en: I'm not concerned about things other than eating and cooking. So, let's talk about food.

t_ch: 嗯！不好意思，我们之前认识吗？
t_en: Oof! Sorry, have we met?

t_ch: 我们应该是初次见面。
t_en: No, we haven't met before.

t_ch: 那我就放心了，原来我们是不认识的人啊，真是不好意思打扰了。
t_en: Then we're strangers — and I'm relieved. I'm really sorry for bothering you.

t_ch: …不过嘛，现在开始认识也不赖。我叫锦余，很高兴认识你。
t_en: ...But we can start to get to know each other now. My name's Jinyu. Nice to meet you.

t_ch: 你已经不记得我了吗？
t_en: Have you already forgotten who I am?

t_ch: 啊！看来我们两个之前是认识的。抱歉啊，我已经彻底忘记你了。
t_en: Ah! We know each other, do we? I'm sorry. I've totally forgotten about you.

t_ch: 虽然我们可能认识过，就把这次当作第一次吧。我叫锦余，很高兴认识你。
t_en: Even though we may know each other, let's treat this as us meeting for the first time. My name's Jinyu. Nice to meet you.

t_ch: 我是{NICKNAME}，很高兴认识你。
t_en: I'm {NICKNAME}, and the pleasure is all mine.

t_ch: …{NICKNAME}。
t_en: ... {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 哈哈哈，原来你是一个容易害羞的人啊。
t_en: Hahaha, so you're a real shy one, huh?

t_ch: 不愿意告诉我你叫什么名字？明白了，反正我也记不住。
t_en: You don't wish to tell me your name? I see. Not like that I'll remember it anyway.

t_ch: 呼——虽然这么说有些唐突，我其实一直在躲避无数个前男友、前女友的「追捕」，我还以为你是他们其中的一位，不过你应该不是。
t_en: Phew... This may be out of the blue, but I'm actually evading the pursuit of my countless ex-lovers. I thought you were one of them, but apparently not.

t_ch: 你有无数个恋人？也太花心了吧！
t_en: You have had countless lovers? Such a player!

t_ch: 你误会了，我其实很专一的。喜欢上一个人，就很难喜欢别人了。
t_en: You're mistaken, my heart can only belong to one. When I like someone, it's hard to like another.

t_ch: 但是这些恋人都是我的前世、前前世、以及前前前世的恋人，我不认识他们，但是他们认识我。
t_en: But these lovers are from my past reincarnations. I don't know them, but they know me.

t_ch: 你怎么知道我不是其中的一员呢？
t_en: And how do you know I'm not one of them?

t_ch: 你要是他们中的一员，你肯定就知道我是什么病情了。但是看着你天真无辜的小脸蛋，我很确信，你根本就不知道。
t_en: If you were, you'd surely know what illness has stricken me. But with that sweet, innocent face of yours, I'm certain you have no clue.

t_ch: 你要是知道了我的特殊疾病之后，你很快就理解了。
t_en: If you know what rare illness I have, you would understand very quickly.

t_ch: 太好了，我们现在就开始吧。
t_en: Great, then let's get started now.

t_ch: 爽快呀！不过，在我们两个确定关系之前，我想提前说清楚一件事。
t_en: Yippee! But before we make things official, I want to make something clear first.

t_ch: 你要是知道了我的特殊疾病之后，再考虑是否拒绝也没有关系。
t_en: Once you know about my condition, it's okay if you decide to reject me.

t_ch: 我们持明族通常生活个数百年就会蜕鳞逆生。身体的损伤会在蜕生过程中重新修补，但记忆也会被完全抹去。
t_en: We Vidyadhara usually stay young by molting after living for centuries. Any damage done to the body will be repaired during the molting process, but our memories are completely wiped.

t_ch: 而我呢，生了怪病，这样五六百年的过程被缩短到了一个月里。一个月之后，我就会忘记我们俩在这里相遇的事情，当然我们的恋爱经历也会消失。
t_en: As for me and my condition, well, my body compacts this five or six-century process into just one month. After one month, I'd have forgotten about our meeting here and now — and this love story, too.

t_ch: 怎么样，听完这些，你理解了吗？这一世恋人我没找到，说不定我们俩就挺合适的。
t_en: How about it? Do you understand after hearing me out? I haven't found a lover in this life, and perhaps we're just right for each other.

t_ch: …可能是有些唐突，不过，你要是知道了我的特殊疾病之后，你也会理解的。
t_en: ...This might be a little rude, but I'm sure you'd understand if you knew about my particular condition.

t_ch: 想再多了解一些你的经历。
t_en: I'd like to know more about your past.

t_ch: 好呀，你随便问问。
t_en: Sure, ask away.

t_ch: 失忆前也是这么浪漫的性格吗？
t_en: Were you always as romantic as this before you lost your memory?

t_ch: 我记不得失忆前是什么性格。不过认识我的人说我无论蜕鳞逆生多少次，我都是这个性格。再说了，如果记忆只有三十天，你也会跟我一样纵情去爱的。
t_en: I can't remember a thing about what I was like before I lost my memory, but those who know me say I remain the same way no matter how many times I molt. Besides, if you only had memories for thirty days, you'd be as spontaneous with love as me.

t_ch: …好像因为前世的我总是热烈地去追求别人，所以我经常遇到一些擅自说着「永远爱我」的人。虽然不好意思，但他们是谁我都记不得了。
t_en: ...It seems that since I was so passionate about romance in my previous lives, I am always meeting people who claim to love me "forever." I'm truly sorry, but I just can't remember who they were.

t_ch: 也许我确实爱过吧！不过那个「我」和现在的「我」根本就是两个人嘛，我才不要去扮演前世去满足别人，假装恩恩爱爱。我要寻找今生今世的最爱！
t_en: Maybe I really did find love! But that version of me is completely different from who I currently am. I don't want to pretend that I'm still that person and to fake my love for others. I'm looking for my true love in this life that I'm living right now!

t_ch: 这么短的记忆，肯定不方便吧。
t_en: It must be a real hassle having such a short memory.

t_ch: 说实话，确实不太方便。所有的朋友，所有的回忆在一个月之后就全部忘记了，好像被全世界抛下了一样。
t_en: To be honest, yes. All my friends and memories just get completely wiped after a month, as though the whole world had abandoned me.

t_ch: 不过，短暂的记忆也并不全是坏事。美好的记忆大多数都很短暂，就像是聚会总是短暂——聚会结束后独处的落寞才会更为长久。
t_en: But having a short memory isn't all that bad. Most of our best memories are brief, just like parties are all destined to end. It's the loneliness between those moments that feels like forever.

t_ch: 要是我像其他仙舟人一样，要面对几百年的孤独和重复…我不敢想象，我这样的性格肯定是受不了的。
t_en: If I had to face centuries of loneliness and repetition just like everyone else aboard Xianzhou... well, I can't imagine someone like me lasting very long like that.

t_ch: 难道说你也有在写日记？
t_en: Are you keeping a diary of all this?

t_ch: 正经人谁写日记啊。你写日记吗？我不写日记。
t_en: Who keeps a diary? Do you write in a diary? I don't.

t_ch: 过去的「我」没有留下什么日记，现在的「我」也不想把日记留给未来。即使一身风流情债难以解释，那也没有什么关系。
t_en: The person I was in the past never kept any diary, and present-day me doesn't really want to leave anything for the future. Even if my debt of love is impossible to explain, I still don't really care for it.

t_ch: 我对每一段感情肯定都是真挚而热烈的，这就已经足够了。如果缘分是真实的，那么就来再次相爱呗，我始终张开怀抱。
t_en: I'm definitely sincere and passionate about every relationship. That's enough for me. If destiny is a thing, then let's fall in love once more — my arms are always open.

t_ch: 我们有缘再见。
t_en: We'll meet again if destiny permits it.

t_ch: 好呀，总觉得我们还会再见面的。
t_en: Sure, I feel like we'll meet again anyway.

t_ch: 我们有缘再见。
t_en: We'll meet again if destiny permits it.

t_ch: 好呀，总觉得我们还会再见面的。
t_en: Sure, I feel like we'll meet again anyway.

t_ch: 等等，你的脸好熟悉…虽说我的记忆只有一个月，但我对你好像还有点儿印象。
t_en: Hold on, you look familiar... I may only have memories from a month ago, but I have vague recollections of you.

t_ch: 就算我的记忆只有一个月，你愿意和我来谈一场说走就走的恋爱吗？你要是想再考虑考虑也行哟。
t_en: Even if my memory only lasts a month, are you still willing to forge a spontaneous romance with me? If you need more time to think it over, that's fine.

t_ch: 哎，小仙只是追求过一阵子新鲜刺激的生活，何至于沦落至此啊……
t_en: *sigh* As a celestial, all I wish for is a refreshing, exciting life. How did it end up this way...

t_ch: 嗯？被人听到了啊…真是丢死个人，本仙我…咳咳，是…是谁偷听我自言自语？
t_en: Hm? I was overheard... How embarrassing. I... Ahem, who... who's eavesdropping on my monologue?

t_ch: 是{F#本姑娘我}{M#本大爷我}。
t_en: It is me!

t_ch: 好些年没有回仙舟了，原来偷听的人都这么理直气壮吗？
t_en: I haven't returned to Xianzhou for some years. Do eavesdroppers now have no shame?

t_ch: 小仙？本仙？
t_en: Celestial?

t_ch: 我只是一时间还没改过口来，请你不要再提起了。
t_en: It was a slip of the tongue. Please do not remind me.

t_ch: 咱们说话小声点儿吧，那边的官老爷们也不太希望我还跟人闲聊的…毕竟，现在小仙我只是一介罪人罢了。
t_en: We should lower our voices. The guards over there don't want me to be chatting with others... I'm nothing but a lowly criminal now.

t_ch: 你犯了什么罪？
t_en: What crime did you commit?

t_ch: 你竟然是囚犯？！
t_en: You're a convict!?

t_ch: 嘘——我都说了，让你小声点儿，这也不是什么光彩的事情。
t_en: Shh! I already told you to lower your voice! It's not something I'm proud of anyway.

t_ch: 小仙我偷偷地逃出仙舟，在外面的世界生活了几百年，才刚被云骑军给抓回来。
t_en: I stole away from Xianzhou and lived for a few centuries in the otherworlds. I was recently captured by the Cloud Knights.

t_ch: 跑去别的世界是很严重的罪吗？
t_en: Is it such a serious offense, running off to other worlds?

t_ch: 你肯定是没有护照偷渡了吧！
t_en: I bet you absconded without a passport!

t_ch: 要是人人都像你这样天真就好了。
t_en: If only the world was as naive as you.

t_ch: 在仙舟，未经天舶司的审核，擅自前往其他世界可是重罪。对你这样的化外民来说可能有些难以理解，你听说过「丰饶之民」对吧？
t_en: On Xianzhou, leaving for other worlds without the Sky-Faring Commission's permission is a serious crime. It may be hard for you Outworlders to wrap your heads around, but you've heard of the Denizens of Abundance, right?

t_ch: 那是受过「丰饶」赐福，到处劫掠、繁衍的长生种联军。他们已经摧毁转变了许多世界。
t_en: Blessed by Abundance, they are allied armies of other long-life species who engage in wanton pillaging and reproduction. They have destroyed and transformed many worlds.

t_ch: 仙舟不允许丰饶孽物滋生，因此也定下了严格的律法，约束长生种的行为。
t_en: Xianzhou forbids the propagation of the Abominations of Abundance, and laid down strict laws to restrain the long-life species' behavior.

t_ch: 所以你想再聊一会儿吗？反正我在这儿等着也等着。
t_en: So you wish to chat a while longer? It's not like I've got anything else to do around here, besides waiting.

t_ch: 好了好了，那边的云骑开始瞪咱俩了，咱们就小声点聊聊，估计他们也懒得管。
t_en: All right, the Cloud Knights over there are starting to stare at us. We should talk more quietly, then they won't be bothered with us.

t_ch: 想再深入地和你聊聊天。
t_en: I want to know more about you.

t_ch: 嗯嗯，我知道你这样的化外民肯定有些好奇的。
t_en: Sure, I know you Outworlders are always curious.

t_ch: 想听你聊聊异世界经历。
t_en: I want to hear your stories of other worlds.

t_ch: …咳，这几百年来，我确实遭遇了很多的事情。至于故事有没有趣，全看你怎么想了。
t_en: ...Ahem, I've had many encounters these past centuries. Whether they are interesting is for you to decide.

t_ch: 我刚逃出仙舟，选了一个文明开化不到一千年的世界。为了避免云骑军的追查，我开着最小型的逃生鞘降落地表。
t_en: Back when I just fled from Xianzhou, I chose a civilization that was not even a millennium young. To evade the Cloud Knights' queries, I drove the smallest escape sheath I could find to descend on the surface.

t_ch: 救命啊，这种蠢事我再也不干了。逃生鞘差一点点就砸到了有人居住的村子。
t_en: Gosh, I'll never do such a dumb thing ever again. The escape sheath almost crashed into a village full of people.

t_ch: 轨道迫降，这种事情应该没人想来第二次。落地时我几乎四分五裂，如果是短生种，当场丧命那是确定无疑了…仙舟人的身体还真是强韧。
t_en: I had to force a landing from the orbit, an experience I bet no one would ever wish to have twice. I was almost smashed to bits when I landed. If I were a short-life species, I'd definitely have been killed on the spot... Good thing us Xianzhou physiques are quite tough.

t_ch: 唔，村子里的人把我给捡了回去。当时联觉信标因为和神经突触间的结合松脱了，在休养了好一阵后，我才搞懂当地的语言。
t_en: Mmm, the villagers brought me back to their village. The Synesthesia Beacon had been dislodged from my synapses, so it wasn't until a long time after my recovery that I could finally understand their language.

t_ch: 他们相信从天而降的我是仙人，叫我「神女」的时候，我觉得吃这点苦算是有回报了吧。
t_en: They believed I was a celestial fallen from the skies, and when they called me a "goddess," it felt like fair reward for my ordeal.

t_ch: 你为什么要逃出仙舟？
t_en: Why did you flee Xianzhou?

t_ch: 我以前只是丹鼎司的一个丹士，做研究能力有限，做医士能力也有限。比我有本事的人，几百年前就显山露水，爬上高位了……
t_en: I was a measly alchemist at the Alchemy Commission, mediocre at both research and medicine. Those more capable than me had already made their marks several centuries ago and climbed the ranks...

t_ch: 在仙舟这种日复一日没啥变化的地方，难道我还有出头的可能吗？
t_en: In a place like Xianzhou where nothing changes, how would I ever get the chance to succeed?

t_ch: 像我这样的人，在仙舟上遍地都是，干着没滋没味的工作，几百年一晃眼也就过去了，然后等着魔阴身发作前，十王司的冥差前来接应。我不想这样啊！
t_en: There were plenty of individuals like me on Xianzhou, laboring away at mindless jobs. Centuries would fly by in the blink of an eye, then you just wait for the Ten-Lords commission's spiritfarers to claim you before you get mara-struck. I don't want any of that!

t_ch: 然后我就想起我平时读的一些闲书，什么转生成为异世界之王之类的。主人公生前有多窝囊，转生后就有多风光，我实在羡慕得不行。
t_en: I then recalled those novels I used to read about reincarnating into a king of some alien world and such. The protagonist would always be a bum in their previous life, but become all-powerful when reincarnated. I truly envied their lives.

t_ch: 所以我想到了一件事——虽然我在仙舟上是个糟糕的丹士，但是在异世界里生活的话，我有着便利的玉兆，还有些做药本事，我还会平平无奇吗？
t_en: So I thought of something — I was a terrible alchemist on Xianzhou, but if I lived in another world, with my trusty jade abaci and talent in medicine, would I still be mediocre?

t_ch: 你真的是犯下了大罪了吗？
t_en: Have you really committed a serious crime?

t_ch: 也不算大罪吧？要不然十王司的审查怎么会排了三个多月都没轮上我。可见我也没做什么大不了的事情。
t_en: I haven't committed a major crime, have I? It's been over three months and it's still not even my turn for the Ten-Lords Commission's investigation. It's obvious that my misdeed was insignificant.

t_ch: 想想也是，我不就是让几只动物会说人话，教当地人种改良的星芋，治好了几个糖尿病患者——虽然他们最后还是土法炼丹害死了自己……
t_en: In hindsight, all I did was make a few animals talk, teach the locals how to plant modified startaros, and cured a few diabetics — though they still ended up killing themselves with primitive alchemical practices...

t_ch: 这么一算，我压根也没干啥嘛！
t_en: All in all, I technically didn't even do anything!

t_ch: 就是这样！我不认为自己是什么重犯，本仙只是在追求自己的幸福罢了！要是能在仙舟过上好生活，谁会想去什么低文明的穷乡僻壤生活啊？
t_en: That's right! I'm not some hardened criminal. I just want to pursue my own happiness! If I could live out the good life on Xianzhou, who would even think of slumming it in some lowly civilization?

t_ch: 为什么囚犯会出现在这里？
t_en: Why would a prisoner be here?

t_ch: 实际上，我也接受审问好些次了…你听说过仙舟俗语「罄竹难书」吗？他们就是这样形容我的，「作案前后跨度过大」，直到现在还没处理完。
t_en: Actually, I've already been interrogated multiple times already... Have you ever heard of Xianzhou saying "one-too-many"? That was how they described my crimes — "great disparity before and after my crimes." They have yet to finish processing my misdeeds.

t_ch: 再加之，这段时间仙舟上出了一些情况，我这样的案件就被暂时延后处置了。
t_en: Something happened in Xianzhou during this time as well, resulting in a processing delay in my case.

t_ch: 所以，我只是在云骑的看管下来星槎海放放风，等收监时间到了，我还得被十王司的人来押回去。
t_en: Anyways, I can only come to Starskiff Haven for a breather under the Cloud Knights' supervision. When it's time for lock-up, I'll still be escorted back by the Ten-Lords Commission.

t_ch: 不聊了，云骑开始瞪我们了。
t_en: We should stop here. The Cloud Knights are starting to glare.

t_ch: 哦…确实，你快走吧，别到时候连你也被抓走扭送十王司了，哈哈。
t_en: Oh... so they are. You should hurry up and go, or you'll end up in the Ten-Lords Commission too, haha.

t_ch: 所以呢，我就找了个星图上都无人问津的世界，找机会私自溜了上去了……
t_en: And that's why I found a world so desolate that nobody even visited it on the astral charts, and took the chance to steal away...

t_ch: 不聊了，云骑开始瞪我们了。
t_en: We should stop here. The Cloud Knights are starting to glare.

t_ch: 哦…确实，你快走吧，别到时候连你也被抓走扭送十王司了，哈哈。
t_en: Oh... so they are. You should hurry up and go, or you'll end up in the Ten-Lords Commission too, haha.

t_ch: 你怎么又来了？
t_en: Why're you here again?

t_ch: 你怎么还没走？
t_en: Why haven't you left?

t_ch: 我也纳闷儿呢，十王司的冥差怎么还不来啊。
t_en: I'm puzzled too. Why aren't the Ten-Lords spiritfarers here yet?

t_ch: 我以为你走了…
t_en: I thought you left...

t_ch: 万幸，我还没走呢，十王司的冥差还没来接我。
t_en: Thank goodness I'm still here. The Ten-Lords spiritfarers haven't come for me.

t_ch: 哎…你有什么事吗？还是单纯想找本仙…我是说，找我聊两句？抓紧说吧，你也知道，我时间不多了。
t_en: So... is something the matter? Or do you simply wish to chat with your honored celestial... I mean, me? Hurry up then. You know I don't have much time left.

t_ch: 想听你聊聊异世界经历。
t_en: I want to hear your stories of other worlds.

t_ch: 本仙…不好意思，咳。这几百年来，我确实遇到了很多有趣的事情。不过最难忘的还是这件事……
t_en: I am... Ahem, sorry. Over the centuries, I have encountered many interesting happenings. But the most memorable one is this...

t_ch: 当时，那个村子附近的小镇上，有一个有钱人。我喊他叫老周。老周有钱，但是人很善良，乐善好施。
t_en: Once, there was a wealthy man in a small town near the village. I called him Old Zhou. He had money, yet he was also kind and helped others in need.

t_ch: 可惜他命不好，生个儿子是天生的智力残障人士。搁仙舟这儿被称为「天缺者」…咳，扯远了。
t_en: A pity that he wasn't very lucky. He had a son who was intellectually impaired. They are what us Xianzhou people call "Incomplete Ones"... Ahem, I digress.

t_ch: 老周听到了我的事，就办了三牲大礼来求我。我一瞧，乖乖，这么隆重的嘛，心里就有些高兴，但仙人嘛，总得端个架子，就说「本仙可以一试」。
t_en: Old Zhou heard of my story and held grand sacrifices with livestock to beg me to help him. I thought, darn, such fanfare! I was secretly very happy, but you know, us celestials have to put on some airs. So I replied, "I shall give it a try."

t_ch: 我心说老周人挺好，得想办法治治他儿子。
t_en: I knew that Old Zhou was a good man, so I had to try to find a way to cure his son.

t_ch: 据说在建木存在的时代里，有不少丹士为了能获得一手资料，会用一种叫解语丹的药物给自己的实验对象提升智力，询问动物感受。
t_en: It's said that in the era of the Ambrosial Arbor, many alchemists — in order to gain knowledge — would feed what's called a Freelingo Pill to their own experimental animal subjects to increase their intelligence, then inquire the animals on their emotions.

t_ch: 你不怕药坏了人？
t_en: You weren't worried about any side effects?

t_ch: 这能一样吗？
t_en: How's that even the same thing?

t_ch: 我心说，人嘛，也就是一种动物。我就拿了几丸解语丹给他的傻儿子。结果那孩子突然开窍叫他爸爸，把老周可高兴坏了。他三请五请，非要供养我。
t_en: Well, humans are also a type of animal. So, I took a few pills to his son, then that child suddenly came to his senses and called out the word "father." Old Zhou was overjoyed. He offered so many times to have me live in his house, all expenses paid.

t_ch: 于是，我就搬到了他家里，和他一起生活了…很多年。老周和我都不喜欢和人交际，于是我就把院里的母鸡和黄狗都变成了「能言走兽」，陪我们两个解闷儿。
t_en: So, I moved into his home and lived with him... for many years. Both Old Zhou and I disliked socializing, so I turned the yard's hens and dogs into talking beasts to help us tide over the boredom.

t_ch: 和老周一起生活的那些年，我每年都比上一年更怕他走。直到他活到一百岁的时候，我才意识到，自己做过头了。
t_en: With every year I spent with Old Zhou, my fear for his death grew and grew. It wasn't until he lived to a hundred years old that I realized I had overdone it.

t_ch: 那时我当然是和现在一模一样，但是老周已经苍老得像是一块蜷缩的木头…他攥着我的手，和我说：「够了，够了。」
t_en: I, of course, looked just as I am now, but Old Zhou looked more like withered wood than flesh... He took my hand and said, "Enough. Enough."

t_ch: 此后我用了三十年，才重新学会了怎么一个人生活。
t_en: I spent thirty years after that learning how to live by myself all over again.

t_ch: 哎！那边那个！聊两句差不多得了。
t_en: Hey! Over there! That's enough talk.

t_ch: 你看他警告咱们了，别的故事有机会再讲给你听吧。
t_en: They're warning us already. I'll tell you about other stories when we get the chance.

t_ch: 想听你聊聊异世界经历。
t_en: I want to hear your stories of other worlds.

t_ch: 你拿我当故事会了？不过我倒是没意见啦…这次就给你讲一个比较好玩的故事吧。
t_en: Do you take me for some storyteller? Not that I mind... I'll tell you something more fun this time.

t_ch: 事迹传开后，很多人来找我看病。有些病我能治好，有些病我只能缓解症状，有些病凭我手头的工具根本无能为力。但不管怎么样，大家都信了我这个仙人。
t_en: After word of my feats got out of the town, many people came to me to treat their illnesses. Some I could cure, some I could only relieve, but some I couldn't do anything about with the tools I had on hand. It didn't matter though, for everyone believed in me.

t_ch: 俗话说得好，浮名惹祸啊。几年后来了个地方官，想查清我到底是什么来路。
t_en: As they say, "fame invites trouble to your door." A local official came by a few years later to investigate my origins.

t_ch: 我本来打算糊弄过去，就请他看看打火机和幻戏。这人倒也厉害的，压根不信什么神仙法术，坚称是我使了障眼法，派兵拘走了我。
t_en: I initially intended to dupe him, so I just showed him a cigarette lighter and some immersia. This man was a tough sell though, and he didn't believe a word of it. He insisted that I resorted to illusive trickery, and had his men arrest me.

t_ch: 我不想伤人，也不敢伤人，就随他去了衙门。他就审我，到底是从哪儿来的什么人。
t_en: I didn't want to hurt anyone, nor did I dare to, so I followed him to the magistrate's court, where he interrogated me on my background.

t_ch: 我很害怕啊，就把实话都和他说了。我说我是天外来客，来自一艘在天上航行的大船，我们这些船上的人都不会死……
t_en: I was so afraid, so I told him the truth about me being a visitor from beyond, that I came from a star-faring ship in the sky, that those on the ship wouldn't age...

t_ch: 他听完，气得暴跳如雷，觉得我还在嘴硬。不过，这人倒是个挺好的人，也没打我板子，只是把我关进了牢里，让我「好好想想自己是谁」。
t_en: When all that was done, he was steaming with anger, thinking I was being facetious. But he was a good man. He didn't dish out any physical punishment, just imprisoned me and told me to seriously think about who I was.

t_ch: 这可是个了不得的哲学问题…
t_en: Now, that's a profound philosophical question...

t_ch: 你老实交代了他也不信…
t_en: He didn't believe you even when you told him the truth...

t_ch: 在牢里我越想越生气，觉得这人真是欺人太甚，我明明说了实话，却还是要把我抓来关。
t_en: I got angrier during my time in prison. This man was a bully! I had already told him the truth, but he still threw me in a cell.

t_ch: 所以我就远程启动了当初带来的两个金人。那地方也没处维修金人，我搬到镇上以后就一直存在老周家的仓库里，舍不得用。
t_en: So, I remotely activated the two aurumatons I originally brought here with me. There was nowhere to maintain them, so I just kept them in Old Zhou's storehouse after I moved into town. They were too valuable.

t_ch: 但这次实在没办法了，我启动了金人。那两个金人过来，面对着传统工艺制造的监狱，赤手空拳就给它拆掉了。
t_en: But now I had no choice but to activate them. They marched into the prison and tore the primitive bars open with their bare hands.

t_ch: 我大摇大摆走出去的时候，看到无论是那些卫兵，还是匆匆赶来的地方官，都惊恐地看着我，不敢上前阻拦。哈哈哈，想起来就想笑。
t_en: As I sauntered my way out, both the guards and the local officials who scurried over to investigate looked upon me with terror, but they dared not stop me. Hahaha, just thinking back on that scene tickles me.

t_ch: 再后来，我在县里的威望越来越强，甚至算是实质上的地方统治者。有一年，那个帝国的皇帝还来找我，求我给他的长子治病。这算是我的高光时刻了。
t_en: Later on, my reputation in the county rose to new heights, to the point where I was the undisputed local governor. There was even a year when the land's Emperor sought me out to treat his eldest son. That was the highlight of my time there.

t_ch: 唉，现在想想，这段人生历历在目，真过瘾。就可惜好景不长啊。
t_en: *sigh* Now that I think back on those vivid memories, that part of my life was truly exhilarating. Alas, good things never last.

t_ch: 哎！那边那个！聊两句差不多得了。
t_en: Hey! Over there! That's enough talk.

t_ch: 你看他警告咱们了，别的故事有机会再讲给你听吧。
t_en: They're warning us already. I'll tell you about other stories when we get the chance.

t_ch: 想听你聊聊异世界经历。
t_en: I want to hear your stories of other worlds.

t_ch: 你给我买杯茶去呗？星芋啵啵就行。虽然难喝，但离乡太久，难喝的东西也变得有滋有味嘞。
t_en: How about buying a cup of tea for me? Startaro bubble tea works. It tastes terrible, but I've left home for so long that I appreciate even the most terrible drinks here.

t_ch: 让我想想，这回给你说说我生涯中最危险、最接近死亡的一次冒险好了。
t_en: Let's see... I'll tell you about my most harrowing experience this time, when I was closest to death.

t_ch: 其实我降落的那个世界里，外来者不只有我这个冒牌仙人。丰饶孽物当然也不会放过那些没有开化，没法保卫自己的世界。
t_en: When I crash-landed on that world, there were already other charlatan celestials there. The Abominations of Abundance would never overlook primitive worlds that had no means to defend themselves.

t_ch: 你听说过「步离人」吗？那是丰饶民中最强悍善战的一支，生有发达的犬齿和利爪，据说与狐人系出同源。仙舟在古代和步离人打过好几场战役呢。
t_en: You've heard of the borisin? They are the most battle-savvy among the Denizens of Abundance, born with full sets of fangs and talons. It's said they share an origin with foxians. Xianzhou and the borisin waged a few wars against each other in ancient times.

t_ch: 那个世界，也有步离人出没。不知是战败的流寇，还是想在那儿实现什么阴谋的斥候，我发现他时，他已经在山里筑起了一个堡垒。
t_en: There was a borisin in that world. I don't know if he was a straggler of a lost war, or a scout plotting some conspiracy, but when I noticed him, he'd already built a stronghold in the mountains.

t_ch: 这家伙拿百姓当实验品，肆意改造，什么令人发指的事情都做尽了。可我只有一个人，压根拿他没法子啊。话说回来，就算带了武器，我也是不敢上的。
t_en: That scumbag used common folk for experiments, modifying them with no regard to their dignity, and did every horrible act you can think of under the sky. But there was only one of me and there was nothing I could do about him. Even if I was armed, I wouldn't have dared to cross him.

t_ch: 思前想后，最后我还是决定豁出去了。要是连相信自己的老百姓都保护不了，那「仙人」还不如留在罗浮做个缩头乌龟好了。所以我……
t_en: I thought long and hard before I decided to give it my all. If I, as a celestial, couldn't even protect the villagers who put all their faith in me, I might as well have stayed behind on the Luofu as a coward. So I...

t_ch: 你拔剑而起！
t_en: You raised the flag of rebellion!

t_ch: 你选择逃避！
t_en: You chose to run away!

t_ch: 呸呸呸！我都说了我打定主意豁出去了！
t_en: Hey! I already said I decided to give it my all!

t_ch: 我潜入步离人在山间筑的巢城，然后给装上了一个发信器。
t_en: I stole into the borisin's mountain city and installed a signal emitter there.

t_ch: 那玩意儿的能量只够发报一次，本是我给自己留的后备方案——真要是搞出什么乱子，还得让云骑军来收场。这下可好，用上了。
t_en: That thing only had enough power for one transmission. I had intended to use it as my last resort — if anything untoward happened, at least the Cloud Knights can get here to clean it up. And now, I had a chance to use it.

t_ch: 要不了多久，云骑军的巡狩部队就来了。他们几乎将那座山的山头削平，确保步离人没逃出去。
t_en: Not long after, the Cloud Knights' hunting squad arrived. They practically leveled the mountain, ensuring the borisin couldn't escape.

t_ch: 我是挺高兴的，但心里隐隐觉得，自己的仙人生涯怕是要到头了。云骑军但凡精细一些，必定能顺着线头抓到我的。唉，果不其然。
t_en: I was pretty pleased with myself, but I knew it was probably the end of my time as a celestial. If the Cloud Knights were any good at their job, they would follow the trail and capture me. *sigh* And so they did.

t_ch: …你看这「罗浮」仙舟上这太卜司的卜算啊，虽然准是准，但那是有规矩的。
t_en: ...Check out this prediction from the Divination Commission of the Xianzhou Luofu. It may be accurate, but there are rules around this stuff.

t_ch: 没错，俗话说「太卜司有三不占」嘛！
t_en: Yep! As the saying goes, "the Divination Commission has three taboos"!

t_ch: 「不沾板，不沾框、不沾网」。
t_en: "Don't touch the board; don't touch the frame; don't touch the net."

t_ch: 怎么太卜司到您这儿就变成体育运动了。人家说的那是「不诚不占、不义不占、不习不占。」
t_en: Why are you making the Divination Commission sound like a sport? They actually say, "No insincere divination, no unjust divination, and no unpracticed divination."

t_ch: 你就别嘚吧嘚了，咱俩往这里一站，大家伙儿看看谁才是太卜司的卜者。
t_en: Quit your nagging. Let's just stop here and leave it up to everyone else to decide who's the real Divination Commission diviner.

t_ch: …哟，合着您是太卜司的卜者？我可真没看出来。
t_en: ...Hang on, you're a Divination Commission diviner? Wow, I had no idea.

t_ch: 你看出来我不就露馅儿了嘛。
t_en: If you could tell, that would give away the game.

t_ch: 我昨日算到风雨将至，结果半夜起风了。你说咱这不就是太卜司卜者的证明吗？
t_en: I predicted a storm was coming yesterday, and just like that, a gale started blowing in the middle of the night. Isn't that proof enough that I'm a Divination Commission diviner?

t_ch: 倒是有点儿风。
t_en: It's just a little wind.

t_ch: 什么叫有点儿风，这风可大了，整整刮了一宿啊！
t_en: What do you mean by "just a little wind" — it was gale force, and it kept on blowing for the whole night!

t_ch: 我怎么没感觉有风？
t_en: How come I didn't feel any wind?

t_ch: 没有风，您是睡在地窖里吗？这风可大了，整整刮了一宿啊！
t_en: No wind? Do you sleep in the basement? It was gale force, and it kept on blowing for the whole night!

t_ch: 咱那神策府门口有几尊石狮子，你记得吗？
t_en: Do you remember how there are several stone lions outside of the gates of the Seat of Divine Foresight?

t_ch: 记得，我之前还拍过一张照片，怒目圆睁、仰天啸啸，看上去非常威严。
t_en: Yes, I even took a picture of them before: Big round eyes, roaring at the sky... they look majestic.

t_ch: 昨晚风可太大了。我本来还在睡觉呢，只听着神策府那边儿传出了哐哐砸地的声音。早上我到星槎海一看，哎哟，有一尊石狮子被风给刮到港口来了。
t_en: The wind last night was roaring. I was asleep, but then I heard the sounds of something being smashed over at the Seat of Divine Foresight. And when I came to Starskiff Haven this morning, gosh, a stone lion had been blown by the wind all the way to the harbor.

t_ch: 那石狮子能被吹到星槎海？
t_en: A stone lion blown to Starskiff Haven?

t_ch: 这我能说瞎话吗？你要不信，你问他去。
t_en: What, you think I'm exaggerating? Go ask him if you don't believe me.

t_ch: 隔着洞天，狮子是怎么过来的？
t_en: How'd the lion come over with a delve in between?

t_ch: 嗨，昨天风不是很大吗？那星槎也被吹走了，这石狮子搭上星槎就来了这里。
t_en: Gah, there were gale-force winds yesterday, yes? That starskiff was also blown away, and this stone lion was carried here by the skiff.

t_ch: 你肯定以为我是在说瞎话是吧？你要不信，你问他去。
t_en: You definitely think this is a bunch of hogwash, don't you? Go ask him if you don't believe me.

t_ch: 您说这风能把石狮子刮到这里吗？
t_en: You say the wind carried the stone lion all the way here?

t_ch: 像话吗！那石狮子那么重那么沉，能让你刮阵风就给吹到星槎海来了吗？
t_en: Nonsense! That stone lion's heavy as a boulder. How could the wind have possibly carried it to Starskiff Haven?

t_ch: 你把这马褂儿给我脱下来吧！
t_en: Get your story straight!

t_ch: 哎！你不是说你不要了吗？你别急啊。那石狮子是神策府的石狮子，有灵性，天冷一刮风，自己就知道换个地儿取暖了，所以它来咱星槎海了嘛。
t_en: Hey! I thought you said you didn't want it! Simmer down, that stone lion belongs to the Seat of Divine Foresight, so it's sentient. When it gets cold from the bellowing winds, it knows how to find a warmer place. That's why it came to Starskiff Haven.

t_ch: 噢！是这么回事。
t_en: Ah, is that right?

t_ch: 您的理解能力可真是高啊。
t_en: Your ability to comprehend things really is incredible.

t_ch: 这马褂儿可真方便，我也得来两件。
t_en: It's a good story. I should use it one day.

t_ch: 没有的事儿，全是胡说八道，这马褂儿给你啦。
t_en: Nah, it's impossible. The whole thing's a bunch of bunk.

t_ch: 那这么大的狮子现在又在哪儿呢？
t_en: And where is this huge lion right now?

t_ch: 哎哟，这不是风停了嘛，它呀又回神策府上班去了。
t_en: *sigh* The wind has stopped, hasn't it? So it went back to the Seat of Divine Foresight to do its job.

t_ch: 谢谢您来跟我俩接茬儿，我叫阿来，他叫阿往，在这儿港口上练习相声呢。您是找我俩有事，还是要咱再给您说一段？
t_en: Thanks for coming to join us. My name's Back'n — and he's Forth. We come to the harbor here to practice our crosstalk comedy. Are you looking for something from us, or do you want us to do another skit for you?

t_ch: 那就再聊个五块钱的。
t_en: Alright, give me another skit worth my penny.

t_ch: 再贵点儿也成。
t_en: I can give something worth a bit more than that.

t_ch: 想听你们再说一段。
t_en: I'd like to hear another skit.

t_ch: 说到咱这儿星槎海，就不能不提司辰宫，提到咱这儿司辰宫啊，就不能不提驭空大人。
t_en: You can't talk about Starskiff Haven without mentioning the Palace of Astrum, and you can't talk about the Palace of Astrum without mentioning Madam Yukong.

t_ch: 我以前见过驭空大人手持弯弓「云鸢铣珧」百步穿杨，那场面真是……
t_en: I once saw Madam Yukong with her Cloudkite Resplendent Longbow, shooting with tremendous accuracy from a hundred feet away. Truly a sight to—

t_ch: 你等等，「百步穿杨」？你说我站在离驭空大人一百零一步的地方，可以用手接住飞箭吗？
t_en: Hang on, "a hundred feet"? Are you saying if I stood <unbreak>101</unbreak> feet away, I could just pluck her arrows out of the air?

t_ch: 「百步穿杨」就是一个文学概念，通常来说驭空大人的箭就算飞行<unbreak>1500</unbreak>米也具有十足的威力。
t_en: It's a figure of speech. Generally speaking, Madam Yukong's arrows are a threat from even <unbreak>5000</unbreak> feet.

t_ch: 那我在<unbreak>1501</unbreak>米能用手接住飞箭吗？
t_en: So you're saying if I stood <unbreak>5001</unbreak> feet away, I could catch her arrows.

t_ch: 你*仙舟粗口*为什么非要接那一箭？
t_en: *Xianzhou profanity* — why do you have to catch her arrows???

t_ch: 你俩来这里练习说相声？
t_en: Are you guys practicing your crosstalk here?

t_ch: 嗨，咱俩都是港口工人，得闲了才来这里练习练习，在别处也不方便。
t_en: Hi! We're both port workers, so we just come here to practice whenever we find the time. It's not really convenient elsewhere.

t_ch: 平时咱们俩都忙，时间老对不上，得亏这个港口也不远，能练上一段是一段。这些本事都是越练越得心应手。
t_en: We're usually really busy and can never find the time. Luckily, the port is close by, so we can squeeze in some practice. It's the kind of thing you get better at by practicing.

t_ch: 说起来，「不夜侯」的西衍先生赏识我们，所以我们有时候也能在茶馆里说上几段，钱赚得不多，主要是赚个经验。
t_en: Speaking of which, Mr. Xiyan over at the Sleepless Earl is a fan of ours, so we sometimes perform a few skits at the teahouse. We don't earn much money, but the experience is priceless.

t_ch: 咱俩以后还得登上更大的舞台呢这不是。
t_en: We are gonna head to some bigger stages for our skits in the future. Right, my bro?

t_ch: 将来你俩就成大明星了。
t_en: You guys are gonna be stars in the future.

t_ch: 曲艺之路还很遥远呐。
t_en: The artist's path is long and difficult.

t_ch: 那我得抓紧机会给你签个名，以后还有机会可以升值。
t_en: Then I'll have to give you an autograph while I can. Who knows how much it'll be worth in the future?

t_ch: 我下次再来吧。
t_en: I'll come back next time.

t_ch: 那成，听我俩说这么久，您也受累了，下次再见！
t_en: Alright, you must be tired after listening to us for so long. Catch you next time!

t_ch: 我来说一段报菜名。蒸羊羔、蒸羊羔、蒸羊羔、蒸羊羔……
t_en: Let me show you what I can do recounting a restaurant menu. Steamed lamb, steamed lamb, steamed lamb, steamed lamb...

t_ch: 合着您这儿就一道菜啊？
t_en: So your shop only has one dish?

t_ch: 哎哟，咱们的观众老爷来了。是找我俩有事，还是要咱再给您说一段？
t_en: Ah, here comes our esteemed audience! Are you looking for something from us, or do you want us to do another skit for you?

t_ch: 建木的事，有什么内幕吗？
t_en: Do you know the inside story about what happened to the Ambrosial Arbor?

t_ch: 啊？不是，{F#姐}{M#哥}，您拿我俩逗闷子呢？
t_en: Huh? Hold on, {F#miss}{M#mister}. Are you making fun of us?

t_ch: 就是，这就有点不地道了，别人可能不知道，但我俩老早就见到过您来找那位——那位谁来着，顶漂亮的狐人姑娘。
t_en: It isn't very nice of you to do that. Others may not know it, but we have long seen — uhm — the gorgeous foxian girl you came to look for earlier.

t_ch: 噢，是停云姑娘，那可是天舶司的接渡使。
t_en: Oh, that's Miss Tingyun. She's not just anyone. She's the Sky-Faring Commission Amicassador.

t_ch: 要我说真有内幕，那也合着也该是您和那停云姑娘门儿清。
t_en: If there were any inside stories, you and Miss Tingyun would know it better than anyone else.

t_ch: 您要是方便，不妨跟我们也透透底儿，甭担心泄密的事儿。我俩嘴严着呢！
t_en: You can tell us a little about it if it's fine with you. Don't worry about us leaking the secret! We'll stay tight-lipped!

t_ch: 关于金人巷的相声录音…
t_en: About the Aurum Alley crosstalk comedy recording...

t_ch: 啊对，这事儿我们俩还没来得及好好谢谢您呢。
t_en: Ah, yes. We haven't thanked you properly for that.

t_ch: 我俩能力一般、水平有限，原本发个录音就当做是玩闹了。
t_en: We weren't any good as crosstalk comedians, so we only recorded our performance for fun.

t_ch: 谁曾想，嘿！反响还真不错！这些日子已经有好些人过来听我们说相声，完事儿以后说是在高阿姨那儿知道我们的了。
t_en: As it turns out, people love it! Many have come these days to enjoy our performance and told us they found out about us through Tall Auntie.

t_ch: 照这样发展下去，我们哥儿俩马上就能摆脱「爱好者」的身份，跻身主流相声界了！
t_en: If things go on at this rate, we'll soon be able to turn pro and be one of the mainstream crosstalk comedians!

t_ch: 阿来，你有点飘了。
t_en: Back'n, I think you're getting a little carried away.

t_ch: 有吗？嘿嘿，不好意思～
t_en: Am I? Hee-hee, I'm sorry about that.

t_ch: {F#小妹妹}{M#小兄弟}，你这是…我明白了，你是来向我引以为傲的肌肉许愿的吧？
t_en: Hey there, {F#lass}{M#lad}. What can I... Oh, I know. You're here to wish upon my marvelous muscles, aren't you?

t_ch: 咦，好恶心。
t_en: Ew, gross.

t_ch: {F#小妹妹}{M#小兄弟}，你看着它的完美的弧线，坚毅的线条，怎么会说恶心呢？
t_en: Just look at these beautiful curves and solid lines, {F#lass}{M#lad}. Why would you ever call them gross?

t_ch: 向肌肉许愿？
t_en: Wish upon your muscles?

t_ch: 啊，没错！你想象一下自己最理想的模样吧，认真向肌肉许下愿望，会实现的！
t_en: That's right. Visualize your ideal physique and then make a wish upon these muscles. It'll come true for sure!

t_ch: 不小心搭话而已，我现在就走。
t_en: Uh, I wasn't trying to start a conversation. Gotta go!

t_ch: 诶？这可不行啊，{F#小妹妹}{M#小兄弟}！
t_en: Not so fast, {F#lass}{M#lad}!

t_ch: 肌肉不会说谎，你会下意识地走到我面前，正是肌肉在渴求运动的结果。
t_en: Muscles don't lie, my friend. Your body moved quicker than your mind and sent you to me because your muscles crave a workout.

t_ch: 你看！肱三头肌，肱二头肌，斜方肌！挺胸，提臀，腹肌燃烧，再来十组，筋肉力全开！
t_en: Behold! Biceps, triceps, traps! Now focus on the form and engage the core! ...Alright, ten more sets! Let's go! Unleash the power of muscles!

t_ch: 俱缓陶醉在肌肉的魅力之中。
t_en: Juhuan has lost himself in the revelry of bodybuilding.

t_ch: 我曾经阅读过学者们对「命途」的讨论，他们说，总有凡人于有意或无意间踏入由「星神」执掌的命途，被称为「命途行者」。
t_en: I once read a scholarly discussion about Paths. They postulate that there will always be mortals that, intentionally or otherwise, set foot on Paths presided over by Aeons. These people are known as Pathstriders.

t_ch: 最原初的人们手无缚鸡之力，但他们锻炼了肌肉，从此便能承受重量，改造自然！你有想过吗，要是在筋肉的命途上走得足够远，人们能做到什么吗？
t_en: The ancients were frail. To overcome that, they trained and built their muscles, eventually gaining the power to bear heavy burdens and reshape nature! Now, have you ever wondered what humans can achieve if they keep advancing on the Path of Brawn?

t_ch: 改造自然的也不是肌肉吧？
t_en: I doubt nature was reshaped by muscles.

t_ch: 看来你脑中的肌肉还不够，还没有领悟到肌肉的境界。
t_en: It seems your cognitive muscles aren't developed enough to appreciate the beauty of brawn.

t_ch: 还能做到什么？改变宇宙规则？
t_en: Hmm, rewrite the laws of the universe?

t_ch: 当然了！只要在这条筋肉之道上走得足够远，就能够改变宇宙规则、逆转时空！
t_en: Absolutely! If we push far enough along the Path of Brawn, we could even rewrite the laws of the universe and reverse time!

t_ch: 银河里真的有「筋肉」命途吗？
t_en: Is there really a Path of Brawn in the cosmos?

t_ch: 也许，眼下是还没有，但是没有人能保证未来它也没有。
t_en: There isn't one yet, but who can say for certain that there won't be one in the future? It's not impossible.

t_ch: 我锻炼至极致的筋肉，就像是一颗黑洞，周围所有的物质都会被它的筋肉引力场吸引，越是强大的肌肉，就越会被束缚在其中。
t_en: Having honed my body to perfection, my muscles are now like a black hole that exerts a gravitational pull on everything around me. The stronger the muscles, the tighter the shackling.

t_ch: 当这股筋肉场达到临界点，我便将开辟全新的筋肉命途，我将成为「筋肉祸祖」！
t_en: When this muscle gravitational field reaches its peak, I shall forge the Path of Brawn and become the Brawn Author!

t_ch: 对了，既然肌肉的引力场在吸引着我们二人，不如我将珍藏多年的独家健身餐配方交给你。这样的话，你也可以一边吃着健身餐，一边锻炼了，就像我一样。
t_en: Since the muscle gravitational field has drawn you to me, I'll be glad to share the recipe for my special workout meal with you. This recipe is something that I've treasured for years. With it, you will be able to train while maintaining a diet suitable for bodybuilding, just like me.

t_ch: 你练块练魔怔了。
t_en: You're becoming a bodybuilding maniac.

t_ch: 那些伟大的事业，在最初的时候总是被视为「疯狂」，我不在乎他人看法！
t_en: The greatest feats are often seen as mania in the beginning. People can think of me however they want.

t_ch: 我也想走上筋肉命途。
t_en: I want to embark on the Path of Brawn too.

t_ch: 筋肉会回应所有锻炼者的呼唤，等我开启了筋肉命途，你的肌肉会感应到共鸣的。
t_en: Muscles always respond to the call of the bodybuilder. Once I pave the Path of Brawn, your muscles will surely resonate with it too.

t_ch: 所以，向肌肉许愿吧！肌肉会回应你，我也会回应你的！
t_en: So, make a wish upon my muscles! They will answer your call, as will I!

t_ch: 向肌肉许愿吧！我将开启新的命途！
t_en: Make a wish upon my muscles! I will blaze a new Path!

t_ch: 这股强烈的筋肉引力，果然是你，我的肌肉又开始亢奋了。
t_en: This strong gravitational pull... I knew it had to be you! My muscles are getting pumped up again!

t_ch: 聊聊筋肉之道。
t_en: Tell me about the Path of Brawn.

t_ch: 筋肉祸祖会满足每一颗肌肉细胞的愿望，问吧，肌肉会告诉你一切的。
t_en: The Brawn Author will fulfill the wishes of every muscle cell. Ask away, for the muscles hold all the answers.

t_ch: 你看起来也不壮…
t_en: You don't look very muscular.

t_ch: 仙舟人的体质决定了，无论我们怎么运动，长出的肌肉都会缓慢消失，回到最初的状态。但这只是表象。
t_en: The bodies of the Xianzhou people are built in a way that we eventually lose any muscle mass we build. Our muscles will gradually revert to their original state, no matter how much we exercise... Or so it seems.

t_ch: 没有东西会凭空消失，肌肉更是如此，它们不过是囤积在了命途海洋的底部，等到肌肉填平了海，筋肉命途就会诞生。
t_en: Nothing disappears for no reason, my friend, especially muscles. The truth is that they're all collecting quietly at the bottom of the Path's ocean. Once the ocean is filled, the Path of Brawn shall emerge.

t_ch: 就在不久前，我还做了个梦，有一个{RUBY_B#漆黑的大日}漆黑的无底洞{RUBY_E#}挂在天上，消失的肌肉都飘进了空洞里。
t_en: Just a while ago, I had a dream where a {RUBY_B#Pitch-Black Great Sun}pitch-black, bottomless hole{RUBY_E#} hung in the sky, and all the muscles that had disappeared were drifting into it.

t_ch: 我相信，那肯定就是我在筋肉命途登神的前兆。
t_en: I believe that to be a sign of me ascending to the Path of Brawn.

t_ch: 如何走上筋肉命途呢？
t_en: How can I embark on the Path of Brawn?

t_ch: 健康的生活方式，平衡的日常饮食，这些都是老生常谈的东西。但真正的秘诀，只有一条——坚持锻炼。
t_en: Old-timers often recommend a healthy lifestyle and a balanced diet, but the true secret lies in one thing: regular exercise.

t_ch: 肌肉不会因为短短一天的努力就出现，能挡住炮弹轰炸的胸大肌，背后一定是久经磨炼的肉体。
t_en: Muscles can't grow overnight. To build strong pecs capable of withstanding cannonball attacks, long-term regular training is essential.

t_ch: 在饭前深蹲，在床上卧推，不要放过任何的空闲时间，哪怕是睡觉，在梦里也要用硬拉和长跑接力。
t_en: Do squats before meals, bench presses in bed... Make every second count. Even when you're asleep, make deadlifts and marathons a part of your dreams.

t_ch: 来吧，让我们一起，筋肉的明天在向我们招手！
t_en: So, join me! A future where muscles shall shine beckons!

t_ch: 为什么来比赛？
t_en: What brings you to the competition?

t_ch: 因为我感受到了，筋肉的共鸣。筋肉有自己的灵魂，它们渴求更强的对手，我只是顺应它们的愿望来到这里而已。
t_en: Something resonated with my muscles. You see, muscles have a soul of their own, and mine crave bigger, more demanding challenges. I'm simply responding to their call.

t_ch: 环绕在竞锋舰的凝聚态筋肉粒子，它们渴望对战和交流，通过碰撞提升自己的筋肉力。
t_en: The condensed muscle particles surrounding the Skysplitter... They crave violence and action. They yearn to clash and collide with others to take their strength to the next level.

t_ch: 哪怕是现在，我的胸大肌和肱二头肌，也在一刻不停地拉扯我的身体，驱使我寻找其他的筋肉引力场。
t_en: Even as we speak, my pecs and biceps are relentlessly compelling me to move, urging me to seek out other muscle gravitational fields.

t_ch: 我想，这或许就是筋肉命途对我的束缚。
t_en: I suppose this is the inevitable destiny of those bound to the Path of Brawn.

t_ch: 以后再聊吧。
t_en: Let's catch up another time.

t_ch: 别忘了坚持运动，肌肉是不会背叛你的。
t_en: Don't slack off on your training. Remember, muscles will never betray you!

t_ch: 还有事，先走了。
t_en: I'm busy, so I'll be heading off.

t_ch: 别忘了坚持运动，肌肉是不会背叛你的。
t_en: Don't slack off on your training. Remember, muscles will never betray you!

t_ch: 「宁可三日无粮，不可一日无茶。」也就是说，仙舟人就算饿着肚子也必须喝茶，我也喝过一些茶，全都没有什么热量，这种说法可真是有点儿意思。
t_en: "I'd rather have three days without food than go one day without tea." I mean, the Xianzhou natives have to drink tea even if they're starving. I've drunk my fill of tea, too — and zero calories, too. It's a pretty interesting turn of phrase.

t_ch: 丹鼎素针、鳞渊春、荫山绿、狩原毛峰…嗯，到底要选什么好呢？
t_en: Vegetations in the Alchemy Cauldron, Whale-Tide Spring, Emerald Hills, and Hunter Maofeng...

t_ch: 要问我的话，就选鳞渊春。
t_en: If you ask me, Whale-Tide Spring's the best.

t_ch: 鳞渊春是吗，稍等，让我查一查茶饮典故。
t_en: Whale-Tide Spring? Hold on, let me look up some good quotes about tea.

t_ch: 「鳞渊春茶芽细嫩，炒成后干茶条索紧结，白毫显露，色泽银绿，冲泡之后杯中白云翻滚，清香袭人……」
t_en: "Whale-Tide Spring buds are tender. Bake-drying results in knotted strips as fine hairs appear with a silvery green hue. Brew to create swirling clouds in your teacup. Its mild fragrance is striking..."

t_ch: 全是我看不懂的仙舟话术，不过没事，点一杯鳞渊春也无妨。
t_en: It's all in unintelligible Xianzhou words. But that's okay, no harm in ordering a pot of Whale-Tide Spring tea.

t_ch: 我其实也不太懂品茶。
t_en: Actually, I don't really know much about tea.

t_ch: 哈哈哈哈，我看出来了，你也是对仙舟文化好奇的化外民。
t_en: Hahaha, I can tell! You're an Outworlder with a curiosity for Xianzhou culture.

t_ch: 在仙舟想要点杯茶可真不容易，生怕是错过了什么文化典故。不过，这只是我个人的坏习惯，{F#小姑娘}{M#小兄弟}你大可不必效仿我。
t_en: Ordering tea aboard Xianzhou isn't the easiest thing, and you might not get all the cultural allusions going on. But that's just my own bad habit, {F#m'girl}{M#boy} — you don't have to be like me.

t_ch: 我看这鳞渊春就不错，暂点一杯也无妨。
t_en: This Whale-Tide Spring is awesome, and I'll sit on a pot of this for a while.

t_ch: 闭着眼睛选一杯不就好了吗？
t_en: May as well just close my eyes and pick out one at random, right?

t_ch: {F#小姑娘}{M#小兄弟}也和我一样是个化外民吧。虽然话说的倒也没错，不过这样做实在是有伤风雅。
t_en: {F#Girl}{M#Boy}, you're an Outworlder just like me. You're not exactly wrong, but it's really quite rude.

t_ch: 仙舟茶饮有历史有文化、有礼仪有规矩，要是随随便便畅饮一杯，总感觉有点「焚琴煮鹤」之嫌……
t_en: Xianzhou tea drinking is steeped in history, culture, decorum, and rules. If one were to glug it down like some common ruffian, it would sully the tea's heritage...

t_ch: 我看这鳞渊春就不错，暂点一杯也无妨。
t_en: This Whale-Tide Spring is awesome, and I'll sit on a pot of this for a while.

t_ch: 我叫霍华德，是星际和平公司的贸易专员，这次来仙舟也是为了一点技术交流的缘分，幸会。
t_en: The name's Howard. I'm a trade specialist for the Interastral Peace Corporation, or IPC. I've come here to trade tech with Xianzhou. A pleasure to make your acquaintance.

t_ch: 幸会，我是{NICKNAME}。
t_en: It's a pleasure to meet you, I'm {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 我是{NICKNAME}。
t_en: I'm {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 你好像很喜欢仙舟文化。
t_en: You must be quite a fan of the Xianzhou culture.

t_ch: 哈哈哈哈，原本我还在母公司的时候就对仙舟文化略知一二，现在有机会外驻了，便提前调查了一下。
t_en: Hahaha, I already knew a little about Xianzhou culture back at HQ. Now I have the opportunity to be stationed here, I have prepared ahead.

t_ch: 这不调查不打紧，越调查越感兴趣…虽然同事们已经叫我「仙舟通」了，但是我了解的还不过是仙舟文化的九牛一毛而已。
t_en: The more I studied it, the more fascinated I became... despite everyone at work jokingly calling me "Mr. Xianzhou," my knowledge about Xianzhou culture really is just a drop in the bucket.

t_ch: 感觉你已经比我懂得更多。
t_en: I think you already know far more than I do.

t_ch: 这是你太客气了。我知道，这就是所谓「谦逊」对吧，「天不言己高，地不言己厚」。
t_en: You're too kind. I know this is the famous "humility," right? "The dragonfruit never sings about its sweetness."

t_ch: 感觉仙舟上处处都藏着绝世高手，但却都装作自己平平无奇，真是个有趣的现象。
t_en: It feels like there are secret masters hidden throughout Xianzhou, each pretending to be super-normal. What a fascinating phenomenon.

t_ch: 我与{F#小姑娘}{M#小兄弟}相谈甚欢，要是有什么想问的，我肯定「知无不言言无不尽」，权当是交个朋友了。
t_en: I've had a great time chatting with you, {F#m'girl}{M#boy}. If there's anything you'd like to know, I'll definitely tell you everything. Consider me your new friend.

t_ch: 哈哈哈哈，你我见面是缘，送你一个小礼物，「礼轻情意重」，请您千万不要客气。
t_en: Hahaha, fate has brought us together. Take this small gift. "Small gifts in place of vast appreciation," as they say. Please accept it.

t_ch: 呃，我又拽仙舟成语了，这是我的坏习惯而已，请不要介意。虽然联觉信标能跨越语言的藩篱，但成语就像潜藏在不同语言里的谜题，令人费解啊。
t_en: Oh, there I go again spouting Xianzhou proverbs. It's a bad habit of mine. Please forgive me. The Synesthesia Beacon may be able to overcome the language barrier, but proverbs are like riddles hidden in different languages — a convoluted quandary.

t_ch: 喔！我听说过，星穹列车又再次启程了对吧？公司的信息简报上提到过这件事了！
t_en: Ooh! I've heard that the Astral Express is starting up again? The IPC's bulletin mentioned this!

t_ch: 想跟霍华德先生闲聊一下。
t_en: I want to chat with Howard.

t_ch: 别客气，那我们聊点儿什么呢？
t_en: Don't be a stranger. What is it you want to talk about?

t_ch: 想打听一点儿仙舟趣闻。
t_en: I want to ask you about some Xianzhou trivia.

t_ch: 我倒是听说了一件趣事，这还是我从那位天舶司主司大人那里打听而来的。
t_en: I heard an interesting rumor from the head of the Sky-Faring Commission.

t_ch: 我去天舶司与仙舟商业代表洽谈的席间，看到驭空大人的爱弓，那真是一柄十足的好武器，不过凑近一看那也并非真正的弓箭。
t_en: I went to a meeting between the Sky-Faring Commission and the Xianzhou traders' representatives, and I saw Madam Yukong's beloved bow. It's really a fine weapon. But upon closer inspection, I discovered it wasn't a real bow.

t_ch: 她那箭矢可以自动制导索敌，开弦不过是为了满足某种古代射术的仪式感。
t_en: Her arrows are actually self-guided auto-aim ones, and drawing the bow is just used to give a sense of antiquity.

t_ch: 仙舟人喜好用一些手段隐藏实力，可能这就是所谓「藏锋守拙」的文化吧。
t_en: The Xianzhou natives like using a few tricks to mask their strength, and it's probably connected to their propensity for humility.

t_ch: 公司来仙舟的贸易活动…
t_en: You came to Xianzhou on a trade mission for the IPC...

t_ch: 哈哈哈哈，这可不兴打听呀。
t_en: Haha, that's not something we can talk about.
